name: MARGARET ACREE
motto: "Believe in yourself, believe in humanity, believe in the success of your undertakings. Fear nothing, you cannot fail."
clubs: Y. W. C. A. Representative to Blue Ridge, '24
description: Bob came to us from North Georgia, bringing all the "pep" and vivacity of a mountain breeze. Not content with finishing in three years, she has taken extra courses in Law and Commerce. That she inspires the utmost confidence in her associates is shown by the many offices she has held. If you want a thing really done—then leave it to Bob. She is a natural leader, and a girl one is proud to follow.

name: LUCILE ALEXANDER
motto: "It matters not so much where you stand, as the direction in which you are moving."
description: A little girl with big blue eyes, golden hair, and a cheerful word for every one? Why that's Lucy. Lucy entered the Freshman class at Shorter College in 1922. Realizing the greater possibilities of a University education, she came to Georgia in 1923. Lucy believes in combining pleasure with work, except just before exams, when she leaves off the latter. This must be a good policy—anyway it brings her many 95's. Lucy possesses the qualities which enable her to make friends and keep them. Her smile is winsome, for it is but the outward sign of a soul that is sincere and strong. Lucy, may you find the way toward the realization of your ideals a happy one.

name: JOHN DANIEL ALLEN
motto: "He who seeks but happiness is a fool; he who denies happiness is a greater fool."
description: An advantage in years and service during the World War gave him a more mature viewpoint than that of most students. He came to the University mistrustful of dogmas and conventions. He believed that the business of the student is to inquire and to criticize, and he employed his pen in exercising his belief. Thereby he helped to enliven the spirit of the student body. Outwardly cold and cynically iconoclastic, to his few intimate friends he was warm—a blend of the practical, the ideal and the rational, with his own share of human qualities.

name: ROBERT EDWARD ANDERSON, B.S.
motto: "Life is what one makes it."
description: Bob hails from the "Garden City" where he completed his preparatory course at Richmond Academy. He registered at Georgia to major in chemistry. Bob is now an assistant chemistry laboratory instructor, which is quite an honor. During his four years at the University, he has won countless numbers of friends, and accomplished a great deal by hard work. Athletically speaking, Bob is quite a tennis shark. We predict for Bob a most successful career.

name: PEGGY BAKER, B.S.H.E.
motto: "It is not things that matter; it is all in the way you look at things."
clubs: President of Y. W. C. A.
description: Bring all the words together that mean dependable, capable, willing and happy withal. Perhaps then it would be possible to express Peggy's character. She is a staunch friend and as one is ever ready to bear a friend's burden. She is the kind of a girl a scared freshman would go to with her troubles, her joys, and her sorrows, knowing that she will meet with never-failing sympathy. Peggy excels in her studies, and her friendliness.

name: YEWLY EUGENE BARGERON, JR., A.B.
motto: "Work not solely for an attainment, but also for the satisfaction of doing your best."
description: Barge came to us with high ambitions; and while here, he has done well in many college activities. He is a hard and willing worker, and is well started on the race that leads to ambitions fulfilled.

name: EITEL BAUER
motto: "Things look mighty bad sometimes, but the thought that someone believes the best in you makes the whole world bright."
description: Pewee has chosen the life of a forester. His keen foresight as a business man, his love of freedom, his realization of the service he will render has induced him into this profession. He loves nursery work because it is but another phase to the work he is to enter. He can't help it because he has so many friends; he was just built that way. Strong of body, clean of mind and ever an advocator of square play in every walk of life, his can be but one final goal—success.

name: WILLIAM DANSLER BEACHAM, JR.
motto: "It is better to be broke, 21, and out of a job than never to have gone to college at all."
description: Bill was cut out to be a student but he wasn't sewed up right. The main thing that he studies about is how to get a golf ball out of a sand trap with his hand without his opponent seeing him. His chief occupation is discussing labor-saving devices while sitting in the front seat of a Ford. Bill is a hunter of note—also of rabbits.

name: CHARLES BERNARD BEALE
motto: "The gist of life, I make no claim; The life I live, my God's to blame."
description: Beale is from Savannah, but he doesn't talk "Geechee." He has his own language and his own thoughts and his own way of fitting deed to word. He has not been frightened by the long arm of professorial authority into becoming a grinding fact machine. His favorite drink is Manhattan coffee. At two-thirty in the morning, he stops smoking and meditating, looks out at the night, and then goes to bed. I would praise him, but he wouldn't like it.

name: DAN SULLY BEELAND, A.B.
motto: "Never room in the same house with a saxophone player."
description: Beely is one of that esoteric kind of persons, who seems a mystery at first sight, but who unfolds himself upon acquaintance. Very seldom do we find a person in college who is neither an athletic star, a scholastic star, or a social star; Beely is just such a person. Believing in himself and in his own happiness, he has refrained from the usual student's habit of attending to everyone's business but his own. But with all his complacency, he has always proven a true and interested friend.

name: DOROTHY REED BELCHER, A. B., Ed.
motto: "Meet the issues of life with a smile, do your best, and let the other fellow worry."
description: A believer in co-education even to the extent of embarking on the sea matrimonial; an energetic and enthusiastic sorority member; a student devoted one hundred per cent to all for which old Georgia stands.

name: ALTUS ROBERT BENNETT, B.S.A.
motto: "Shoulder arms, make no retreat; Fight well Life's battles, but take well defeat."
clubs: First Lieutenant, R. O. T. C.
description: Those who know this young man will appreciate his companionship. You at once realize his persistent ability in whatever work he undertakes. We don't know what his future is, but we are sure that, whatever the conditions are, we will find him one of the most prominent leaders in his community. We wish him the best of luck and happiness in whatever profession he chooses.

name: BERTHA MARGARET BENSON
motto: "If thou faint in the day of adversity, thy strength is small."
clubs: Junior Representative on Soule Hall House Council, '24-'25
description: A recognized leader in everything she undertakes, her service has been demanded where responsibility was to be met and she has responded to the fullest extent of an exceptional ability. She has combined with her leadership a deep and understanding sympathy not found in those who are less considerate of others. Her friendship is a valuable possession, for such sincere friends are indeed rare. There will be many who will miss her cheerful smiles.

name: JOSEPH WILSON BERRY
motto: "A telephone pole never hits an auto except in self-defense."
description: Dahlonega's loss is our gain. Bill's reason for changing was that he did not like mountain life, but we have a vivid idea that it was because Milledgeville is a few "bumps" nearer. The inmates of the ladies' institution at Milledgeville hopefully await his arrival—per Ford—ever so often. To the heretofore common belief, "No man can be a sheik and a scholar simultaneously" we here take issue—and cite a living example in the person of this man. Being so successful along this line, we venture to prophesy for him a bright future.

name: SARAH LOUISE BETTS
motto: "To be rather than to seem; to do rather than to dream."
description: To those who know her, nothing is necessary to be said about Sarah. She came to the University as a first honor graduate of Lucy Cobb, where she won recognition, not only in literary fields, but also in athletic events—and she has kept up her record here. Sarah's sweet disposition and modesty of manner have won her many friends wherever she has gone. Sarah is a shark in French, too, and when Professor Lustrat says: "Dites-moi ce que vous avez lu, Madamoiselle Betts," she can always respond with excellent French passages. In fact, she is pretty, witty, original and wise—a thousand girls in one.

name: CHARLES ALLEN BICKERSTAFF
motto: "Two heads are better than one—when they're on the same shoulder."
description: For four years Charlie has adorned the campus. He has been interested and successful in almost every branch of college activity. An efficient manager of athletic teams, a popular companion, a consistent student, a leader in military and other student activities—we shall miss him as he matriculates into the greater University of Life, but we do believe that his foundations have been well laid. We predict a gentlemanly fortune for this gentleman of fortune.

name: HARRISON AGNEW BIRCHMORE
motto: "To thine own self be true, And it must follow, as the night the day, Thou canst not then be false to any man."
description: In '23, Billie came to Georgia as the "Athens High School's Baby," a title given him because he was the youngest member of his class, and such a small boy. Billie does not seek high places. It is by a knowledge of his sublime vision of justice and duty—by a knowledge of his life—that he is exalted in the hearts of his friends. He will study law at Georgia and later at Harvard. Having the essential qualities for success, an honest, industrious nature, combined with high aspirations, he will be fitted for a life of usefulness.

name: JOSEPH E. BISHOP
motto: "Cash is the jack of all trades."
enrolltime: beginning of his college career at the North Georgia Agricultural College
clubs: Student Chapter; American Society of Civil Engineers
description: An engineer and a soldier, a rare but happy combination is the most fitting phrase in the description of such a subject. Learned in the art of war, he has fought his way to the front and over the top, keeping the opposing forces riveted to the ground in humble subjugation. After finding himself in the beginning of his college career at the North Georgia Agricultural College, he entered the University with the goal in view which he is now just on the verge of reaching. A full-fledged, hard-working civil engineer.

name: GEORGE NORMAN BISHOP
motto: "Never begin anything you can't finish."
description: First seeing the light of day in Winder, Doc came early in life to breathe the atmosphere and study in the schools of "The Classic City." He entered Georgia, therefore, already familiar with much of the tradition which becomes a part of the first week's training of every freshman. His friendly attitude immediately won for him an enviable circle of friends who have continued throughout his course, together with new-made ones, to respect him for his integrity of character and versatility of accomplishment.

name: MORDAUNT GREY BOATWRIGHT
motto: "Where can it be, this land of El Dorado?"
description: We all believe that silence is golden, but few of us test it, and still fewer are millionaires. That's the rub, for here we have one of the holders of that intangible wealth. Still calmer, almost too much so. If a hundred-mile gale caught him in a bateau a thousand miles from shore, he'd probably look a little worried and casually hope for better weather. "Boaty" is destined for big things because the little ones fail to attract his attention. "Boaty's" chief literary accomplishment is "My Adventures in College, or Three Years in the Q Room."

name: BERTRAM SIDNEY BOLEY
motto: "Thoughts of great deeds were mine, dear Friend, when first The clouds which wrap this world from youth did pass, I do remember well the hour which burst My spirit's sleep, a fresh May-dawn it was."

name: MILDRED BOLEY
motto: "Just as you sow, so shall you reap."
description: Solomon spoke of women who were so wondrous wise. Tennyson spoke of all their charms, and praised them to the skies. Homer sang in accents bold about their loyalty, But to describe our own dear Mildred—that's the task they left to me. I could easily duplicate Solomon, Tennyson, and Homer, for application to my subject, but they would not suffice. They have not made mention of dependability, industriousness, and a pleasant disposition, which are Mildred's most attractive qualities. We are very fond of you, Mildred.

name: WAYLAND EUGENE BOLTON
motto: "Simplicity, Sincerity, Service."
description: To know "Sam" is to be his friend. Though quiet and reserved, he has underneath the surface that something which draws one to like him. He does not believe in the artificial flourishes which some use to get along in this world. When he tells you he'll do something for you, that something is done. At all times, he is willing to go much out of his way in order to help a friend, at the same time standing firmly for his rights. He is a man who considers character as his greatest asset.

name: JAMES CALVIN BONNER
motto: "There's something big in every little thing; find it. There's something little in every big thing; find that."
description: Bonner is a gentleman in the finest sense of the word; gifted with a personality which immediately commands the respect and confidence of all. Quiet and unassuming, a thinker rather than a dreamer, generous to his adversaries and true to his friends, and possessing a genial good nature, that is Bonner. For Pete we predict great success in the pursuit of his chosen profession, journalism.

name: CLARENCE HARVEY BRAND, B.S.A.
motto: "I care not for women; let them care for themselves."
description: Mr. Clarence Harvey Brand's college career began in 1923. From the first he has been an outstanding student, his average being well above the 80 mark and by finishing this year he has successfully and creditably finished four years' work in three years. In addition to his scholastic record he has been prominent in other branches of college activities. He is well known among his fellow students who expect him to keep up the excellent record that he has made at the University.

name: JAMES NEWTON BRAWNER, JR., B.S.
motto: "There is no substitute for thorough-going, ardent, and sincere earnestness."
description: Jimmie is the prince of good fellows, an unassuming chap with natural force. The leadership figuratively seems to ooze forth without an effort. His scholastic records prove that he is nobody's dolt. In surveying the purview of his activities one sees they are not limited. Jimmie's harnessed ambition to be an M. D. seems to have guided his way through college. Johns Hopkins will be fortunate in getting an A scholar and a gentleman—ready to carry his brunt of life and to alleviate some of its suffering.

name: ANNA DEANE BRIDGES, B.S.H.E.
motto: "A blotter is the thing you spend your time looking for while the ink is drying."
description: An outstanding student in every respect. Studious, charming, attractive, original in her thought. An asset in any affair undertaken. She masters all with an ease and grace that pronounce her a genius. Honors, to her, are merely incidents and are not considered "thrills that come once in a lifetime." To know her is a pleasure. To talk with her is inspiring.

name: MRS. JOHN MELL BROOKS
motto: "To be, rather than to seem."
description: Mrs. Brooks came to the University as a senior this year. Before coming here, she was at Spartanburg, S. C. She shows in her attitude toward study and by her interest in educational progress that her work as a teacher is going to be of real value to her pupils. We shall expect many of our future citizens to be inspired to higher lives because of her guidance and inspiration. She is courteous, refined, and helpful to everyone. Life must hold for her a rich and full experience.

name: LEWIS PEARCE BROWN
motto: "Don't steal the thunder of the big tent. Have a show of your own."
description: "Buddy," a collegian, a student, an Athenian. Which brings us to the conclusion that he must be a pretty good sort of a fellow. Though, like many other Athenians, "Buddy" has failed to take an interest in college activities, he has not failed to make many friends who realized that below his misanthropic aspect "Buddy" carries an altruistic heart. To send "Buddy" off with wishes and hopes would be an insult. We'll just expect to meet a success about ten years hence.

name: RAIFORD FRANKLIN BROWN
motto: "Always get what you go after."
description: Raif's heart lies in depths of forest land unbounded. He loves the woods, and has elected to spend his days among them. His greatest joy is in taking his dog and gun for a day's hunt after partridges. He is admirably conspicuous at any social gathering, but had much rather spend a quiet, happy night at his "shack." A truer heart never beat in a more manly breast, and he commands the respect of all who know him. Nature has called him to her field of service, and with joy unrestrained and unconcealed, he has answered.

name: MRS. ANNIE MAE WOOD BRYANT
motto: "Give the best that you have to the world and the best will come back to you."
description: "Persistence wins the dip"—Annie Mae is one of our Home Demonstration Agents who has labored hard and long. She is an intelligent student, and ambitious to accomplish wonders along the lines of nutrition. The fact is evident, that she has done, is doing, and will do, great things. Her joy in service has mellowed the difficulties along her high road and brightened the promise of the future, so that she carries responsibility without the slightest ruffling of her feathers. We can't think of anything but success for you in any line of future activity, and may your many attainments find expression in your chosen field of service.

name: JOHN HERBERT BURROUGHS
motto: "Success will come to you if you strive hard enough for it."
description: John Herbert Burroughs, the man from Ila. It may be a small town with a small name, but not so with John. John is a hard worker and has shown the makings of a man while in school. No, you did not hear so much about him while here, but he was working to reach the high ideal he has set up. He has a sincere purpose; so to him, as to others like him, will come success. He is a fellow whom we that know him are glad to call our friend.

name: DAVID STANLEY CAMPBELL
motto: "Take it all in, consider it, believe what you please."
clubs: Student Chapter, American Society of Civil Engineers
description: An engineer with ingenuity, a soldier of distinction, a ladies' man who is admired as well as liked, a man's man who may be depended upon, a buddie to be desired. His ability has won him a place at the top among those who toil by night and day within the environs of Moore College.

name: LOYCE FURMAN CARGILE
motto: "That which is worth having is worth fighting for; the harder the fight, the more enjoyable the reward."
description: From childhood, those with whom Loyce has come in contact have been impressed by her perseverance, clear insight, mental alertness and independence. This independence allows her to accept no favor that carries with it an inevitable obligation. When she decides to do a thing, she pursues it with astounding quickness and accuracy. Her course in the University is a fair example of this. All that her mind seems unable to grasp is the meaning of impossibility. If she has respect for the individual, she must have great respect for herself. She might easily say with Whitman: "Henceforth I ask not good fortune; I myself am good fortune."

name: GEORGE SLADE CARGILL, JR.
motto: "Conventions are only the deprivations of personal liberties."
clubs: Winner of Abney Trophy in Hunter Class, Second Place, Best Student Rider, Third Place, Pair Jumping, R. O. T. C. Horse Show
description: Though the road was rocky and rough, his cheerful smile was always present. In dormitory football he stood out as Candler Hall's star end. His cups and ribbons at horse-shows and as a cavalryman stand as witnesses to his prowess with horses. "Show me a man who loves a horse, and I'll show you a man," was never truer than in this case.

name: THOMAS McELMURRAY CLOSE
motto: "A bird in the hand is bad table manners."
description: Working on the theory that all work and no pay makes a school teacher, Tom has started out early to "instruct the young." And, of course, all his students love him. In fact, he numbers his friends by his acquaintances. By constant application and hard work, etc.—(Boloney—now laugh that off). Indeed that's just what Tom will probably do, for he has that happy capacity of laughing off anything that disturbs his "piece" of mind.

name: VICTORIA BILLUPS COHEN
motto: "Too low they build who build beneath the stars."
description: Victoria came from Wesleyan in '25, and she has won many laurels at Georgia. Besides making an excellent record in class work, she has taken part in all college activities. Her hobby is writing, and she is a distinguished member of the journalism department. Yet, she believes in having a good time along with work. Vickie will be greatly missed because of her charm and attractiveness, and her ability to make friends. She's little, but she's all there, with a ready smile for everyone.

name: FRANCES ELIZABETH COMER
motto: "Whatever may fall my lot in life, may I do it with all my might."
description: Frances possesses one of those choice personalities that simply cannot be described; like a rare flower that modestly radiates loveliness and perfume, without any effort. She is so genuine, so true and so fitted, yet never seems to realize that she is superior, or talented above the average. One only has to taste the products of her culinary art to realize her natural ability. If she were on a desert island, she could manage to concoct a delectable meal. She is a wonderful girl to have around. "Let Frances do it" is what every one says when it comes to arranging banquets and dainties.

name: RALPH COLLINS CONNALLY
motto: "True greatness consists not of never falling, but of rising after each fall."
enrolltime: Ralph came to us two years ago
description: Ralph came to us two years ago, small in stature, but during his span of days here, we have found him endowed with a masterful intellect, and, better still, those characteristics which will will always enshrine him in the memories of those of us who have known him during his brief but colorful stay here. "Shorty" is receiving a two-year degree. His thirst for knowledge may bring him into our midst again. Should he fail to return, we shall be the losers, so we solemnly raise our glass of pre-war stuff and, in the ancient collegiate vernacular, say: "Here's to you and your return."

name: FRANCES LUCYLE COOK, B.S.H.E.
motto: "All that glitters is not gold."
description: Upon entering Georgia, Lucyle chose to work for a B. S. H. E. She has been very successful in her undertaking, for in June she will receive not only the B. S., but also the H. E., if she chooses. "Cyle" is both popular and pretty but not conceited; dignified yet "peppy;" easy-going, yet determined. She is likewise amiable and frank. These traits, coupled with a sterling character and high ideals, make a rare combination. With such a versatility of good traits, we are sure she will be successful in whatever she undertakes.

name: GEORGE WARREN CUMBUS, JR., B.S.C.E.
motto: "It is surprising, really, how few murders take place in college."
description: This old "war-horse" of a college generation now passes on seeking new fields to conquer. It is past the understanding of his class-mates, how G. W. can do so much and still steer clear of the hibernation into which the pressure of an ever-increasing burden of work to be done almost inevitably forces even the best of us. The records of the past assure that the leap from college life into the hum-drum of a busy world will mark the beginning of a great career.

name: ELIZABETH CURRY, B.S.H.E.
motto: "Life is yours; make of it what you will."
description: Beneath an exterior of calm and sweetness we ever so often catch a glimpse of a steadfast purpose and a determination to accomplish some good. But what? We can only guess for Elizabeth is ever reticent about those things which mean most to her. "And can she bake a cherry pie, Charlie Boy?"—We'll say she can, and sew, too, for those four years on Ag Hill have accomplished much. She has come via stepping-stones of sandwiches into the hearts of many acquaintances, but her friends are hers for her charm of personality and her willingness to serve.

name: CLARA GIBBS DALLY
motto: "To be doubtful—but never of one's self."
description: "Dally," although having only been with us since September, 1925, has already shown us those qualities of enthusiasm, earnestness and conscientiousness in study that lead us to expect her to become a real scholar. She has high ideals of honor, justice and fairness, an unshaken faith in humanity, and a broad optimistic outlook upon life in general. These qualities, coupled with her tact, sincerity and pleasant manner, will go far in making for her a happy, useful and successful future.

name: MUNROE de MERE d'ANTIGNAC
motto: "The best things are the hardest to reach, therefore let no difficulties hinder you."
description: From the very beginning of his college career, "Jackie" proved himself to be an exception. After completing his work here in three years, he spent his last summer in school in Wisconsin, where he specialized in Co-operative Marketing. His ability as a student as well as in other college activities is due far more credit than we are able to give him in these words. His ambition and uncommon personality have certainly been recognized, by those who have known him, and have caused everyone to look up to him with the highest respect. The many friends he leaves behind wish him the best of luck and feel quite confident that he will bring glory to Old Georgia.

name: FORDE DARDEN
motto: "Always be true to the best that is in you."
description: Coming to the University of Georgia with a diploma from G. S. C. W., where she was a part of everything on the campus, Forde will go away from Georgia with much more than her B. S. H. E. and the things for which it stands, and she will leave on the campus a lasting impression of what the right kind of Co-ed really should be. To the casual, she may appear reserved and indifferent, but it doesn't take long to get underneath and find the real Forde. Her cheerfulness, willing helpfulness, sociableness, ambition and unending energy will win for her the success which is her due.

name: ALWIN WIMBISH DAVID
motto: "Fish are found only in deep water."
description: "Al" is one of those steady, quiet, unassuming fellows whom you can depend upon on all occasions. He does not advertise his abilities, or boast of his learning, but when results are to be measured, he is always among those present. Modesty, dignity, integrity of character, and unalloyed hard work are some of the qualities which have won for "Al" a permanent place in the confidence and esteem of his fellow students. We feel sure that with his habits of consistent work, his tendency to look on the bright side of things, and his unfailing determination, he will meet with success.

name: JOSEPH QUENTIN DAVIDSON
motto: "In framing an ideal we may assume what we wish, but should avoid impossibilities."
description: Underneath a quiet, dignified and reserved exterior, you find the characteristics that account for his popularity and success. He is sincere, earnest, generous, broad-minded, and dependable. He goes into any undertaking with vigor and whole-heartedness and does not quit until he has thoroughly accomplished what he set out to do. His outstanding ability is due to a brilliant personality shining through good humor, cheerfulness, and willingness to do.

name: DOUGLAS LAFAYETTE DAVIS
motto: "The way you think and act in this life shows your worth to civilization."
description: The hills of Habersham gave us in the fall of '22, this loyal son of whom they can feel proud. His ready smile, his hearty greeting of good cheer, and his love for fun has made him a host of friends. His hair-raising stories about France, and those that happened up home have made him very noted in this art. He is a "Two Minute" man when it comes to the opposite sex, with whom he is very popular. Doug, we hope and feel assured that you will make a success as a veterinary surgeon.

name: JEWEL DAVIS
motto: "All real strength and success comes from our reliance on some power higher than human."
description: In September, 1925, a tiny little girl entered the registrar's office and timidly asked: "Is this where you register?" She was told that the University students registered there, but the grammar school was several blocks away. But Jewel registered senior. Jewel is the kind of a girl to whom flattery is an enigma. There is nothing of the veneer or tinsel about her. Her ideals are of the highest nature, and she has strength of character to live up to them. In spite of her love of arguing, she has the unusual ability to see the other fellow's viewpoint, and as a result, she is beloved by all who know her.

name: RUFUS STERLING DAVIS
motto: "Nobility may be inherited, but virtue must be acquired."
description: To overcome handicaps resulting from service in the World War was not the least of his tasks when he entered the University. Not only did he overcome them but completed in three years the course usually requiring four, and did it with credit to himself and to his Alma Mater. Loyalty to his friends, sincerity, a desire to learn—these are among characteristics he has manifested and for which he will be remembered.

name: GARR SAMUEL DENNIS
motto: "Give consideration to the ideals of others, but possess some originality."
description: Dennis entered here three years ago. He had previously spent a year at the University of Tennessee. Coming well prepared to pursue collegiate work in Agriculture, he has done well. He is interested in every phase of his subject. He does not have very much to say because there is so much to do that he would like to do well. He is very careful in his work. He takes into consideration all that has happened in the past and uses it as a basis for what may happen in the future. He profits by the mistakes he may have made and tries to prevent their recurrence.

name: CHARLES PATRICK DOHERTY, B.S.F.
motto: "I have striven and done my best; I am satisfied, ready, and waiting."
enrolltime: Four years ago, Doc came out of the army to the University
description: Four years ago, Doc came out of the army to the University, conscious that he had assumed the responsibility of an education. He has ably attained the latter. Doc is replete with rich, Irish wit. A "bull" session with him is worth one's time. He grins at hard work, for he is not a shirker. When the wind blows ill, he has sand enough to laugh out loud. It matters not how you feel about him, Doc is always your friend. All in all, he's a fine fellow you can't help but like and respect.

name: JUDSON HARRELL DREWRY, B.S.C.
motto: "Make up your mind what you want to do—then do it."
description: "Rat" is one person who lives up to his life's philosophy. When he makes a decision, he sticks to it—not as a dolichocifalous or a tyro might do—but as one who knows that he is right. Life to him is something more than a game to be watched otiosely from the sidelines. It is a contest in which we are all participants and wherein each must first choose his part with acumen, and then live up to that part.

name: DOROTHY DRISKELL, A.B.
motto: "Smile, and the world smiles with you."
clubs: Junior Representative, Women's Student Government Association
description: Indeed the letter "D" must be Dorothy's magic letter, for most of her qualities center about it. She is a dainty damsel, dapper, and delicately delightful. The best part about her is that she is a thoroughly normal person, never too dazzling, or deep, never downcast, drab, or dull, but always desirable, because of her equable disposition. She has made an enviable record in all of her studies, and hosts of friends among the students, without regard to age, sex, or previous condition of servitude.

name: WILLIAM HORACE DUNNAWAY
motto: "Wise is he who knows nothing of women."
description: "De," your friends are going to miss you, a lad of indomitable will, keen foresight, jovial nature, intense power of concentration, "but a man for a' that." No one realizes the fact that "Bill" has burned the midnight oil more than I do, having seen him come in nights without number and begin studying at 11:45. Horace, we're sure you will sail a straight course, be master of your fate, and captain of your soul, but we fear you are guided by two bright eyes rising over a sea of tears and are being driven by the winds of chance and will eventually anchor in the troublous sea of matrimony.

name: ELROY DuPUIS
motto: "When bigger fools are to be had, colleges will get them."
description: So meticulously exacting, yet so grossly blind, is this fellow, that he would be oblivious to a cyclone while he pondered upon the advisability of a comma or a semi-colon. A peculiar fellow, who heaps insults upon Morpheus by night and lavishes idolatry upon the same god by day. One who procrastinates, yet completes. Genius hides in eccentricities—let's hope for the best.

name: DAVID LEWIS EARNEST
motto: "Being shot at sunrise is a great thing if you can afford it."
description: "Hammer's" splendid personality—an affable smile and a fine sense of humor—has made him one of the best-liked men in college. At all times he has been able to enter into the various phases of college life without any neglect of his duties as a student, and he has made of himself a well-rounded man. His unselfish nature and his high ideals will certainly be invaluable to him in any line of endeavor that he may choose as his life's work.

name: JAMES LLOYD EATON
motto: "When God sorts out the weather and sends rain, why rain's my choice."
description: A driver of oxen and woods-worker from the tall timber of Tennessee; a jolly good forester with a wit as keen and wholesome as a frosty morning on a tree-covered mountain; a clever hand with the cross-cut saw and a dexterous user of the double-bitted axe; a familiar figure of tardiness, who constantly dreads work undone; a laugher-away of trivial trials; a buckler-to with a will; a beloved dispeller of anxieties, and a hearty friend of courage.

name: ROBERT BAXTER ENGLAND
motto: "Life is never too short for courtesy."
description: I take pleasure in introducing you to one, Robert Baxter England, better known as "Great Britain." You have often heard of the "Man of the Mountains;" here he is. England finished his agricultural course in three years, which is a high recommendation to his scholastic ability. He is a man of his own convictions, and stands by them until proven false. Few have heard of him, but England works while others play.

name: JOHN ETHEREDGE
motto: "Be not the first by whom the new is tried, nor yet the last to lay the old aside."
description: After finishing his prep school career at Tifton A. and M., John selected a life companion. A few years after the honeymoon, he became desirous of acquiring more knowledge, and moved his family to Athens and entered the University. After remaining two and a half years, he began teaching and has followed this occupation up to the present time. Determined to finish, John completed his college work by attending the summer schools. We wish for him continued success in the educational field.

name: CLARENCE SAMUEL FAIN, A.B.
motto: “Heed thine own reproaches, but fear not those of any other.”
description: Having been in close association with Clarence for four years we have come to love and respect him, not because of any publicity or small honor notoriety, but because by his words and actions he has always shown himself to be a gentleman and a true friend. He is quiet, modest, dignified, and unassuming, and he goes the even tenor of his way content with the assurance that honor and fame come to him who acts well his part. His pleasing personality makes for him scores of friends, and he is bound to succeed in any undertaking.

name: JAMES CHOICE FANNING, A.B.
motto: “Fame follows those whom she is unworthy to guide.”
clubs: First Lieutenant, R. O. T. C.
description: With a constant, engaging smile, Choice has, with diligence and without gusto, endeavored to learn something about the “why, whither, hither and hence” of life. Placing no premium on honors he has manifested the true traits of a student, holding reflective thinking as essential to learning, and being ever tolerant of criticism and diverse opinions. Always cheerful, kind, and unusually individualistic, he has developed a lovable personality and a philosophy of gentle texture, making himself endeared to many as a worth-while friend.

name: JAMES LAURITZ FORBES, B. S. C.
motto: “Life is short, make the most of it.”
description: “Bo Mac” is both a ladies' man and a man's man. He is so successful in the former that several freshmen have been caught playing Sherlock Holmes, attempting to discover his secret. He is a man one enjoys being with, for he is not only jolly but is always dependable. His record speaks for him and although he may soon be gone from among us, his deeds and influence will remain with us for years to come.

name: GORDON AULIE FRANKLIN
motto: "I live not in myself, but I become Portion of that around me."
description: Byron, with a prophet's eye, must have looked through the years and seen Gordon, for this sentence could apply to no one more aptly than to Gordon. He weaves himself, unostentatiously, but surely, into the lives of all who know him. Always in good spirits, genial and cordial, he has gone through the University apparently without a care, forming the greatest number of contacts with the least amount of friction. His many friends, his good scholastic record, and his general fineness mark him a well-rounded man.

name: HAROLD LLOYD FREDERICK
motto: "He who makes no mistakes does nothing."
description: A steady, plugging spirit, an indomitable will that rises to noble heights to overcome serious obstacles, and, to crown it all, a personality that fairly exudes true and constant friendliness. All these are characteristic of Friday. His level head is not turned by the fiery passions of the hour, but, unperturbed, he pursues the even tenor of his life. He is one of the most popular men in the Engineering School. In future years, many friends in other spheres of life, will cherish and bless the happy memories of collegiate experiences with this cheerful fellow.

name: LUCY BOOTON FREDERICK
motto: "Contempt is a feeling you have for the people who are not like you."
description: Two years ago Lucy came from Hollins to the University to acquire Georgia culture. So now she is a person of taste, discrimination and cleverness. Besides these traits, she has another to be envied, one making for some distinction since so few can boast it—she has a sense of humor, sparkling with originality. One might add sweetness and wholesomeness—but, oh, well, why mention such unspeakable banalities when one can talk of frankness, of sincerity, of grace and charm unequalled!

name: WALTER ALBERT FULLER, B.S.A.
motto: "Ambitions were never hatched while sitting idly by the way."
description: It is a pleasure to write about a man who strives so earnestly and steadily toward the goal of his ambition. With high ideals and a determination to attain them, he has laid well the foundation for future achievement. A sincere friend beyond price, and a truly honest man to be admired. Walter has made a special study of chickens (both kinds) and may well be considered an authority on the subject. We predict for him a bright future, and a well-merited success in his field. May joy go with him all the way.

name: ROBERT LEE GARRETT, B.S.
motto: "Show what you stand for and are by deeds, not creeds."
description: Bob came to Georgia two years ago, having graduated at Bowdon College. He registered temporarily as a sophomore, but has graduated despite the obstacles. Bob's chief forte is math, and it is an impregnable one. Not being satisfied with the undergraduate math courses, Bob has taken two courses in graduate math. His grades in those, as in all his subjects, would credit to any graduate student. Bob believes that hard work accounts for success. Consistency and sincerity describe him. Nothing superfluous about Bob. Success to you, Bob.

name: JOHN BULLOCK GASKINS, B.S.F.
motto: "Look not too closely at the faults of others, lest you show the world one of your own."
description: John, like old Abe Lincoln, is long, tall and a hard worker. And although John has never written upon a wooden shovel with charcoal, he certainly does his stuff upon paper with a typewriter or a pen. Patrick Henry said: "Give me Liberty, etc."; John says: "Give me trees, etc." He is an excellent student of lawyer ancestry, and a true son of southern soil.

name: THOMAS HART GIGNILLIAT, B.S.A.
motto: "Live! for the morrow has greater things in store for you."
description: Thomas emigrates, not from his Alma Mater, Emory Academy, not from his home, the young Pineorian metropolis, but directly from the cotton patch he glides in to study his chosen profession. Not only a great judge of live-stock, but also of the proper utilization of time. He temporarily renounced the farm during a season of high water with the aspiration of becoming a master producer, so that he could flush the market with cotton when the water receded thereby reducing the price of shirts. Thomas leaves a multitude of friends who will vouch for him, and our only pleasure in his departure is the vast possibility of his future.

name: CHANDLER BALKCOM GLADIN, B.S.A.
motto: "Where there is a will, there is a way to success."
description: Ladies and gents: You would never suspect on looking into the face of this modest looking young man that he would be such a great ladies' man. It is, alas, too true. Compared with him, Don Juan was a piker. Fearful mothers rush wildly into the street to rescue their beautiful daughters whenever he goes by. What the future holds in store for him we cannot say, but because of his wild habits, we fear for the worst.

name: THOMAS WRIGHT GOODWIN, B.S.
motto: "Life is a mass of details; let's take time to enjoy the fundamentals."
description: Men of old sung of an emperor who blissfully played a violin as the great city burned. Men of today tell of a youth who indifferently strummed on a banjo for three long years, while his companions searched desperately, but vainly, for a rare jewel called knowledge. In spite of his apparent disdain of all things pertaining to the serious side of life, Tom's record in the classroom is among the highest, and he is preparing to enter that most difficult, but noble profession—medicine.

name: JOHN FRANKLIN GRAHAM, A.B., Ed.
motto: "Happiness is not to be found in rest, but in striving and achieving."
description: Curriculum to many is a monster which interferes with college life and worries fathers, but to John it has been an opportunity to add to his store of knowledge. If he shows the same qualities in after life that he has shown in college, his life is sure to be as successful as his college career. He is not only a good student, a hard worker, but best of all—a true friend.

name: JOHN WILLIAM GRAY, JR.
motto: "If women interfere with work, quit work."
description: To one who looks into things deeply, Jack is truly one who merits the term "man." He is quiet, unassuming and perhaps different from most of us, but in his own way is a top-notcher. Seeing that his philosophy is to put woman above everything else, we can be sure that Jack's wife will be some snappy lady. We wish you luck, Jack, and the best of it through the years to come.

name: THOMAS STEPHEN GRAY, B.S.C.
motto: "Inconsistency shows thought."
description: Here is a paradoxical fellow—a thinker without pessimism; a doubter but not a cynic. But thinking and doubting—like polo—with Tom are venturesome by-play; he takes them with a disarming smile. Say this for him: He will take life as he finds it, explore it with a fearless, light heart, and find enormous fun in the living.

name: JONES LLEWELLYN GRIFFIN
motto: "Know what you want and then go after it."
description: "Grif" has been a familiar figure for the past four years. The hard-working student, the stellar trackman, the dignified soldier—all these are going to be missed when "Griff" leaves us. Some will remember him best in one field; some in another. His comrades of the cinder paths will recall the fighting spirit that carried him to many a win; his fellow sufferers in military will remember an awkward rookie who rose to the top; his fellow students in general will remember an earnest, serious, good natured boy. (He was so loyal to the University that he would go only with go!) For four years he did well among us, and we expect him to do the same among others.

name: PENELOPE GRIFFIN
motto: "Let me live in a house beside the road, and be a friend to man."
description: When Penelope leaves the University, her diploma is not all she will carry away. During her three years here, she has won our hearts and our admiration because of her friendliness and dignity. She is not only sincere and faithful to her friends, but she has the proper attitude towards her work. She has made a splendid record, yet she has found time for social activities. She is dignified, yet not serious; sincere, yet not too reserved—she is in every way a valuable friend.

name: ERNEST LEE GRIGGS, JR.
motto: "Labor with what zeal we will, something still remains undone."
clubs: Student Chapter, American Society of Civil Engineers
description: Lee's four years have been well spent. Not content with the required engineering course, he has carried extra subjects and has maintained an excellent standing. In every field he enters, he is soon at the top, and is recognized as a leader. His cheerful, bantering manner is appreciated by the many who know him.

name: ADLAI BEE GRINER, D.V.M.
motto: "Not what you do or say, but how many will be sorry when you pass away!"
description: After serving two years in the Navy during the World War, "Bee" came to the University in February, 1923, with the determination to accomplish two things; namely, obtain his degree, and make some lasting friends. He has more than succeeded in both of these ambitions. His friends are too numerous to count, and he has done well in his work. His example as a student has been an inspiration to his fellows. The highest hopes for your success shall ever be present in the hearts of your fellow-students.

name: EDWARD WATERMAN HAGLER, B.S.
motto: "Treasure traditions, but do not let them prevent progress."
description: When someone whom everyone likes comes to college the sophists usually shake their heads, convinced that he will be no good or too good. Occasionally they are disappointed. "Ed" came to Georgia a fine boy and will leave a fine man, with the highest expectations of numerous friends. They are sure he will always stand for liberality without violence, constructiveness without staleness, decency without falsity, and in short, a combination of qualities that will win him admiration and affection wherever he goes.

name: ZONA HAMILTON, A.B.
motto: "Be good and you will be admired; don't and you will be envied."
description: Although Zona's name begins with the last letter of the alphabet, let no one think her the last in anything—far be it from her. Not to know her argues yourself unknown. Zona possesses the Zeal of a Zouave, the Zest of a Zephyr, and we expect her to attain the Zenith of Zeus. A dash of Zip and a total absence of Zero characteristics make her a bright, interesting and thoroughly enjoyable person to be with.

name: FRANK CONLEY HARALSON, B.S.C.
motto: "Look to the future and not the past, for the best is yet to come."
description: "Senator" hails from the hills of Blairsville. When he came here from N. G. A. C. he brought a most highly developed personality which has won the friendship of just about all the worth-while people here. In him, we find strength of character and a delightful personality, which help to make him an honest and upright gentleman. His scholastic record is far above the average, and in the busy, big world, we feel sure that he will always be numbered with those intelligent, useful, and gentlemanly citizens whom we all like to know. Haralson, you have the best wishes of a huge number of sincere friends.

name: WILLIAM HENRY HARDMAN, B.S.A.
motto: "Don't put a thing off, put it over."
description: "Hard Charlie" came to the University in the fall of 1922, and has applied himself diligently for four years. A steadier man with a stronger determination to succeed in the service of mankind will not be found in the pages of this annual. He came, he saw, he conquered. Now he is ready to take his place among the horticulturists of his state and further the progress made in that industry. Here's to your success, "Hard Charlie." A man filled with your desires and constancy of purpose is bound to succeed.

name: JAMES BRUCE HARPER, B.S.C.
motto: "The great business of man is to improve his mind and govern his manners."
description: Bruce is a man whose sincerity of purpose, unselfishness and high ideals have made him admired among all who have known him. He has worked faithfully in the classroom, completing a four-year course in three years; yet he is an honor student. He has found time, nevertheless, to enter into the many phases of college life—and to enjoy them. It has been an inspiration really to know the man.

name: JOHN WILLIAM HARRIS, JR.
motto: "Our greatest virtue is never in being defeated but rising each time we fall."
description: Four years ago this young man had a wild desire for a higher education and a "G." Those four years are past and now he can boast of an exposure to the former and an ownership of the latter; in other words, he has gratified his desire in a most satisfactory fashion. The gratification of such a desire has been a task not to be scoffed at and here's hoping that the task he encounters in the future will receive the same tone of treatment that this one has, because if they do, success for him will be written with capital letters. Incidentally, we prophesy such lettering.

name: GEORGE RAIFORD HATFIELD, D.V.M.
motto: "Refined sense and exalted sense may be good, but I think common sense is the best sense of all."
description: George Raiford Hatfield, from Monticello, Ga., entered the World War in 1918. He served his country with credit. September, 1922, found him matriculated at the University of Georgia for a degree in Veterinary Medicine. He made good from the start, and here he is the finished product. He has made an enviable record, and numbers his friends by his acquaintances. He is always full of life, and dependable as well as loyal to every trust.

name: TYLER FRANCIS HAYGOOD, B.S.C.
motto: "Life is a tragedy of which we are the victims."
description: Haygood is a quiet, unassuming fellow; not the one to proclaim aloud his own worth. It is there and you will see it easily for yourself. In three years he has finished his course with some distinction, although he has not used up all his hours exploring deeply in his lesson books. He has explored other books, and people, too, to find other things than bank accounts, flat or puffed-up in man's life. He has taken to himself some of the best things the University could give him, and rejected the worst. His illusions have fallen from him, yet he is without bitterness; he is a commerce student that does not dream nightly of heaped-up money bags.

name: JOHN JOSEPH HENNESSY
motto: "Life in toto is incomprehensible."
clubs: President, Vice-President, and Critic of Demosthenian
description: Ah! Demosthenes, thy shade should hang its head in shame. Thou, Spartius, didst but boast with child's talk. Thy listening gladiators would have spurned thy pleas as infant prattle, could they but have trembled with awe at this master of eloquence. Ye gods of logic, philosophy, and science, burn thy silly scrips and meekly retire.

name: ELISHA MERRIMAN HERNDON
motto: "Base all things on facts, disregard traditions, but have faith."
clubs: Squadron Adjutant, R. O. T. C.
description: "Lige" is one of those happy-go-lucky mortals who think, yet refuse to worry. One who manages to hide his thoughts with superficialities. If a Bolshevist threw a bomb into his back-yard, he'd probably move out hurriedly and laugh at the spectacle of a house blowing up. Gilded brass is enticing, but usage detracts from its beauty, while nickel-plated gold grows beautiful with wear. "Lige" reminds us of something like the latter.

name: MARY WYOLINE HESTER
motto: "Insist on yourself; never imitate."
description: If a woman possesses understanding, no greater thing can she have, and all the embroidered cloths of heaven would not be too much to lay at her feet. Wyoline rushes into life, picking here and there, and she nearly always picks the best, as she has learned the distinct art of discrimination. Yet when she makes a mistake in her choice, broken lutes are not pondered over; she merely chooses a better one next time as she goes gypsying along.

name: POPE RUSSELL HILL, M.S.
motto: "Exactness."
description: All the little details of life which appear so troublesome to the most of us, willingly yield their charming intricacies to Pope. So interested has he become in the minutiae of life that mathematics is his hobby and unintelligible jumbled figures are a feast for him. Pope is a member of that very exclusive type of unostentatious successes which, now and then, honors a university by enrollment. Without advertisement the faculty found him and now he frightens little freshmen with great big formulae.

name: CHARLES HERTY HOOPER, A.B.
motto: "It is not what you think or say, but what you do that counts."
description: Charlie Herty Hooper—perhaps some of you know him as Major Hooper—is the kind of a fellow we all like to have as a friend. He is always there, when a job is to be done, with his clear thinking, open-mindedness, strong will power, and determination to do the job right. He is steady and true to his convictions, but always willing to listen to the other fellow's side of the question. Charlie will win out at what he undertakes, so we know a great future lies before him.

name: WILLIAM CARL HUGGINS, B.S.A.
motto: "Vivere sat vincere."
description: Quiet and unassuming, beloved by those who have met him in the paths of duty, Carl has spent four years at Georgia and honored his Alma Mater by his earnest endeavor in the classroom. He is the son of science, but to know the real man is to see him on the winding Ogeechee, back to Mother Nature, rod in hand, entrapping poor fishes. Free in the broad expanse of South Georgia, the man lives, and there is revealed the life of the man. We do not wish Carl success; he will command his own.

name: LULA MAE INGRAM, B.S.H.E.
motto: "Work to do the greatest good to the greatest number."
enrolltime: After spending one year at Piedmont College, Lulu came here
description: She openeth her mouth with wisdom; and in her tongue is the law of kindness. After spending one year at Piedmont College, Lulu came here, determined to get her degree from dear "ole Georgia." Lulu is always ready to enter into any kind of wholesome fun, yet she's never found to be unprepared in the classroom. Her poise, dignity, cheerfulness and wit demand the love of those who know her. She makes friends easily and keeps them long—she means well. There's a work waiting for Lulu to do; we gladly give her up in order that she may do it. Luck to you, "Lulu."

name: GEORGE SAYNOR JOHNSON, B.S.C.
motto: "Trust your friends, but cut the cards."
description: We are indebted to Augusta and Richmond Academy for what we are wont to call our ideal combination—athlete, splendid student and, above all, an individual possessing those sterling qualities which characterize a real man. He is the type of regular fellow whom one would look to when in search of a princely friend and companion. With the impetus given him by Richmond, Skeet has made the best of his stay at Georgia and has in three years taken unto himself a commercial degree and though he is only one of the long lineage who have received the degree here, the best of the lot may well rise and heartily welcome George Saynor Johnson to the Round Table.

name: JOHN WESLEY JOHNSON, M.S.C.
motto: "Be honest, and though the world proclaim you false, you will yet have friends."
description: "Little Johnson" came to us from Dalton High School just four years ago, but in this short time, he has received two degrees. Yes, he has well-earned them, too, for he has worked hard and made a high average, showing his ability to be of the highest calibre. He has not had time to go out for all the college activities, but he has been found busy with many and varied labors, which will be of great help in the business world. We prophesy for Johnson a busy life, full of happiness, success, and service.

name: Janie Jones
motto: "They are never alone who are accompanied by noble thoughts."
description: Janie is a thorough student, capable of much, and much we predict she will accomplish. Her charming personality, keen sense of humor, cultural interests, and intelligence make her a desirable friend. Janie is always calm, never at loss for repartee and is happiest when she is doing a favor for somebody. She has managed to do two years' work in one, read nearly all the books in the library, and has still had time to make a host of friends. Her ability to make quick decisions, an unlimited amount of energy, and a purposeful endeavor assure her success in the future.

name: Mallary Jones
motto: "Satisfaction comes from worth-while things."
description: After spending two years at Sullins, Mallary came to the University and here she has made good use of opportunities. In addition to making a splendid scholastic record she has taken part in athletics and other college activities. Her cheerful disposition and live, enthusiastic interest in worth-while persons and things have brought her valuable returns. She knows what she wants, does not allow her temper and moods to govern her and acts on firm principles. Once she undertakes a thing she leaves no stone unturned to accomplish it. With all the splendid possibilities she possesses her future should be crowned with success.

name: Otis Warner Jones
motto: "It is not what you do that counts so much, but how you do it."
description: "Buck," as he is known to his host of friends, hails from Atlanta. After attending the Seventh District A. & M. School, he served four years with the Marines, and sixteen months of this time in France. After letting the Red Cross nurses restore his health, Buck felt equal to a course at Georgia. He has proven his ability during the last three years. His smiles and efficiency in handling a car seem to attract the eyes of the feminine sex. We wish for you much success.

name: RALPH KELLY JONES, B.S.C.E.
motto: "Be yourself."
description: Sturdy, persistent, and dependable are the three adjectives which best describe Ralph. During the four years here, he has kept just one jump ahead of the hard working class of engineers, leaving behind him a record of speed and accuracy. Although extremely interested in the ladies, he has always answered the call of duty first, and this is the attribute which will bedeck his path to glory with the gems of accomplishment.

name: RICHARD MORDECAI JONES, JR., A.B.
motto: "Lay your diamonds aside when you go slumming."
description: Here is a young man who has encroached upon idealistic adaptation. Dick is one of those few fortunates who can be the life of anybody's party, whether it be a congregation of angelical hymn chanters, an assembly of wits, or a symposium of collegiate demons. The strange feature of this personality is that with all its attractiveness it remains untainted by University politics. For as Dick once said: "I joined a club during my sophomore year, but that was before I learned better."

name: JAMES ATTAWAY KENNEDY, B.S.C.E.
motto: "A stout matron is a lovely girl gone to waist."
clubs: Student Chapter, American Society of Civil Engineers
description: At the end of a career lasting through four years of toil and struggle with the hardships of an engineering course, "Jim" emerges from the shadows of his Alma Mater not only interested in his profession, but a full convert to the diversions which lend flavor to the business of living. The one fault from which he persistently refuses to abstain is that of praising his home-town, but it seems hopeless after four years. This old home-town now awaits his return with anxiety, and "There's a reason."

name: MARTIN EDWARD KILPATRICK, B.S.
motto: "God pity the man without self-respect and pride, But the devil take the man to whom egotism is tied."
description: The years have rolled on and again Georgia has one who is worthy to be called gentleman, athlete, and scholar. Early, Buster saw the true principles of life, and he has followed them without deviation. There being no compromise with honor in him, but a rigid adherence to right as he saw it; those loose in principle themselves have not always characterized him as broadminded. Perhaps Buster might best be described by a remark once casually made of him by a mere acquaintance: "He has attained more prominence than anyone ever here, to have sought none."

name: ARCHIE LANGLEY, B.S.A.
motto: "What is this world without friends?"
description: Kid Langley is said to possess the best disposition on the campus. Versatile in his accomplishments, but at the same time constant in temperament; a genius in work, but not a follower of that phantom, "honors;" ever ready to serve, but never presumptuous; having a personality coveted by his friends, and yet, willing to share it with his companions. Kid has not only been benefitted by his University, but has, in turn, benefitted his University. The University gladly endorses such products as having come from her own folds.

name: LEEROY LEFFLER, A.B.
motto: "The play's not the thing; fair play is."
description: We have with us this evening, ladies and gentlemen, a youth who does not believe in advertising—himself. In many respects he is slow, but in most, quite steady. "Lef" was born in Savannah, August 7, 1906. He prepped at Savannah High, where he played football and baseball, and entered Georgia three years ago. He succeeded in making his "G" in football during the past season, and has been interested in several other activities while here. Finishing at the age of nineteen, "Lef" has a long, and we hope, a most successful future ahead of him.

name: HERMAN J. LESSER
motto: "Be it ever so humble, there's no place like Rome."
description: As the Romans of old left home to conquer Athens, so Herman left his native city. How well he has succeeded, is evidenced by his record in the University as a budding pharmacist, by his ability, little short of genius, at keeping a fraternity house in a livable condition, and by the host of friends he has made. The qualities of versatility and perseverance, so essential to a full and complete life, he possesses in great measure. We predict for him a brilliant career in the field of his chosen endeavor.

name: FLORENCE WELTNER LESTER
motto: "She who hesitates is old-fashioned."
description: Florence Lester is a girl who is the four C's that every woman should be: clean, clever, companionable and chic. She is a good sport in the best sense of the word, with a quick understanding, great capacity for enjoyment and just the right amount of foolishness which should round out all choice spirits. She has humor without cruelty, and a sense of fun without any tedious round of inconsequential inanities. We are safe in forecasting a pleasant life for Florence, as it will be chiefly occupied in getting and giving enjoyment.

name: MARSHALL CRAIGG LEVIE
motto: "Ability and 'bull' are a great combination, but if you can have only one, let ability go."
description: Four years ago an aggressive youth, nicknamed "Blondie," entered the University. He entered this institution in high gear and no curve has been too sharp for him to take. His political career evidently began in his infancy, as politics have been on the boom since.

name: MORRIS HARRY LEVY, B.S.C.
motto: "Work like the devil, and you'll find plenty of time to play."
enrolltime: During three years, Harry has completed a four-year course
description: The right proportions of silence and wit are found in few, and here we have one of the few. Harry can put on a most awe-inspiring countenance when there is work to be done and do it. But when the work is complete, you'll go a long way to find a more enthusiastic player, funster, or wit. During three years, Harry has completed a four-year course, but not at the expense of his education. For he has delved moderately in all those things that make up a "college man."

name: WALTON FELKER LEWIS, A.B.
motto: "Never do today what you can put off until tomorrow."
description: This motley world of ours is composed of three types of people, specifically divided as follows: First, that exacting cut-and-dried mechanical type who awakes by the clock, goes to all meals on time, prepares for his tomorrow's classes tonight, and faithfully believes that tomorrow never comes; second, the in-between person who is just so-so, nothing individualistic about him, truly a type; and third, that colorful, charming, important sort of a fellow who never does anything on time, who wanders in after the intermission and looks as if he's been missed, and who doesn't turn in his senior write-up on time because he thinks tomorrow will do. Felker is king of all those who belong to the last group.

name: FRANCES ELIZABETH LITTLE, A. B.
motto: "So little done—so much to do!"
clubs: Joe Brown Connally Scholarship in Georgia History
Junior Representative, Woman's Student Government Association, '24-'25
description: In Frances we have an exception to the general rule that everyone is deficient in at least one branch of education. Whatever the subject, her marks top the list. But Frances is more than an honor student. Always well disposed, refined, and helpful, she has made many friends in her quiet and unobtrusive manner.

name: THOMAS DONALD MARKS, B.S.
motto: "Never kiss a pretty girl through a bottle of ink."
clubs: Staff Captain, R. O. T. C., '25-'26
description: For four years, Tom has been battling them. During three years he fought to get on top, but for the past year he has needed only to stay there. In military, in boxing, on the track and cross-country teams, and on the rifle squad, this diminutive fellow has met the best of them and they are his. If success in college, if a cheerful fighting frame of mind can aid one in life, Tom should go far.

name: BESS MATTHEWS, A. B., Ed.
motto: "If you see good in everybody, nearly everybody will see good in you."
description: Bess has made for herself a host of friends during her two years at the University. She has a charming personality, remarkable ability, and a determination that is unsurpassed. She attempts the impossible and makes it possible. Our hope is that Bess will make as great a success in life as she has made in college, and we feel sure that she will, because "a cheerful disposition is a fund of ready capital; a magnet for the good things of life."

name: JOHN ALFRED MAULDIN, B.S.A.
motto: "Bear ever in mind that life is what we make it."
description: In September, '23, Mauldin leaped off the Seaboard with a pair of saddle bags and inquired his way to Candler Hall. His first years were characterized by conspicuous bashfulness, a studious attitude, and high scholastic achievements, but his last one, by heroic approaches upon the fair sex and orchestral aptitudes. Truly, Alfred is an apt student, most congenial, and in every way a likable fellow, worthy of all confidence. The world is certain to be bettered by his accomplishments.

name: CARLTON NEWTON MELL, B.S.C.
motto: "I care not to be loved or wooed, My motto is, give me food."
description: Here we have the only person in captivity who has not a worry. Come what may, "Cotch" remains the same, with the same old smile that has won for him the friendship of the entire student body. From his freshman year, he has been the sensation of the glee clubs. Playing the role of "end man," he has brought forth inevitable laughter and applause from all audiences, even including our beloved Dean Dudley, who, it is said, breaks forth in joyous mirth when Cotch begins his antics. Seriously though, we expect to see Cotch rise to heights unknown—what with his unchangeable pleasing personality, his alert mind, and his nonchalance, there is nothing that can keep him from climbing the ladder of success. Here's to you, old boy, you've "got um."

name: ELLA SUE MINOR, A.B., Ed.
motto: "Life holds no greater pleasure than that which comes through serving others."
description: No one would know from Ella Sue's face, manner, or conduct that she has ever experienced a sorrow or had a care. She has a joyous voice and a sunny face. She never passes a class-mate without a cheerful greeting. While at the University, Ella Sue studied eagerly and intelligently, digesting and assimilating all that she absorbed. Her kind and generous heart, her steadfastness and courage, endeared her to all. Her influence on those who met and watched her and listened to her was far-reaching. She epitomizes in her small body all that makes woman lovable and man supreme: Honor, Faith, Love.

name: RICHARD GIDEON MINER, B.S.C.E.
motto: "Say what you please, but be careful where you say it."
clubs: Captain, Troop D, R. O. T. C., '25-'26
description: For four years Dick has been equally at home in the saddle, on the parade field, behind the transit, and in Number Four, Candler Hall. There are rumors of a girl somewhere, but such reports are, in Dick's own words, groundless. However, there are certain things that aren't rumors. Three shining cups won at a certain horse show, the captaincy of a troop of Cavalry, the reputation of being able to chase contours successfully, an Engineering diploma, membership in the American Association of Engineers, the best wishes of many friends—these show the qualities that Dick will match against the world.

name: ANN LOUISE MOON, A.B.J.
motto: "And large blue eyes that flash on you a volley Of rays that say a thousand things at once." "To live!"
description: Byron certainly must have had Louise in mind, for she does love to talk, and if you are around her enough she will say a thousand things. The best part is that she really does say something; generally something startling, and beneath it there is something intrinsically fine about her. In her two years here, Louise has become as well known as any girl in the University, and we dare say it will be the same wherever she goes.

name: MRS. PEARL CAMPBELL MOON, B.S.H.E.
motto: "Let me live in a house by the side of the road, and be a friend to man."
description: A constant source of inspiration and joy is the very presence of "Honeymoon." With indefatigable energy and ambition unparalleled, she has placed her name among the few who crown the summit of college achievements. She is emblematic of all that is best and highest in womanhood; true to her convictions; true to her friends; just when a judgment is to be made; dependable when needed. Her ideals are the highest, and she has the strength of character to live up to them.

name: ANDREW CECIL MOORE, B.S.C.
motto: "Make today's work better than yesterday's."
description: The Georgia enthusiasts who have seen Scrappy on the gridiron or diamond know that his nickname well suits him, for there has never been a harder fighter, on any field, than Scrappy. He rose into the immortal class when, in the fall of 1924, he kicked a field goal against Vanderbilt in the last few minutes of play and won a game that will live always in the memory of those who love the Red and Black. Colonel Andrew has established an enviable record, and with it has won hundreds of friends who will watch him in his battles of the future, with the same interest and heart-felt hopes of success as they have in the past. A real athlete and a sterling gentleman—Luck to you Scrappy.

name: ANNE ROBERTS MORAN
motto: "Why worry? Things will happen anyway."
description: To the casual observer, there are two striking features about Anne: Her ability to make friends quickly, and her downright out and out cuteness. Many times she has inspired a boy to say to another: "Introduce me to that cute little girl." A winning smile and bright repartee complete the effect of making people want to know her. And after you know her you discover the finer qualities. Sincerity and genuineness are rare virtues, but then Anne is a rare person.

name: JAMES ZEB MORRIS
motto: "He who would master others must first master himself."
description: Zeb is a man of no mean ability. Exhibiting from childhood a love for music, he has distinguished himself in the pursuit of his hobby. Possessing an element of genius he is not single-tracked. His fondness for music has not hindered him in his quest for learning, he having made an enviable record in all of his classes which well deserves the praise bestowed upon him by his instructors. Possessing a genial personality, in which humor and affability and other good qualities combine themselves in pleasing proportions, Zeb has made hosts of friends who wish him well.

name: CASSANDRA ELIZABETH MORTON
motto: "Though we travel the world over to find the beautiful, we must carry it with us or we find it not."
description: Generally speaking, Betty is a true, all-round girl. She possesses a keen sense of humor and besides enjoying laughing herself, she wants to make other people happy, thereby gaining many friends. Her other qualities number among such things as a distinct musical talent, a love of nature and beauty in environment, manner, and thought.

name: JAMES WHITE MORTON, JR.
motto: "A thing of beauty is a toy forever."
description: Behold! The Vicar of Winterville, commonly known as the "Squire." Like Walter Camp, Squire attributes his success to a daily dozen. Six days out of the week, Squire travels six miles from home to school, and six days out of every week, Squire travels from school to home again. Jim's outstanding quality is his generosity. He will give you his last shirt, and as quickly forget it. Such generosity we fear, does not predict a success in the business sense of the word, but we freely predict that whatever he loses in dollars, Jim is equally sure of gaining in friends.

name: SUSAN MOSS
motto: "The way of the transgressor is interesting."
description: "Dumps" is one of that vast horde of modern American misses who bob their hair, have suppressed desires, imaginary complexes, and care-free manners. But "Dumps" is not a type, for she has very distinctive claims to individuality in her inevitably disheveled auburn hair, and a frown that isn't a frown at all. Epicurus would have philosophized a little more had he met "Dumps," for his philosophy is far from complete without hers.

name: PHILIP ANTHONY MULHERIN
motto: "If she be not kind to me, what care I how fair she be?"
description: Above is Philip, a widely known and even more widely discussed personage. The "Duke's" renown is well merited; Philip is the musician, the physician, the gentleman, and the man. Is he not worthy of praise and renown? Philip has fought bravely, and usually he has won; it has been on rare occasions that the "Captain" has been forced to retreat. We wish Philip his just dues. With those, great as they shall be, he shall live in true greatness.

name: HELEN McDORMAN
motto: "Let every eye negotiate for itself and trust no agent."
description: The English language is, unfortunately, rather limited, and its limitations are the more keenly realized when so meagre a space is allotted for a description of Helen. Helen is a friend, loyal, sympathetic and true, whose friendship is not simply in the abstract, but is ever ready to show itself in most practical form. Her scholarship, too, is typical of her, being far above the average. She is an enthusiastic supporter of all college activities. All who have known her will forever remember all that the name of Helen McDorman signifies.

name: RUBY LOUISE McMAHAN
motto: "Where there is life, there is hope."
description: Great was the loss of Lucy Cobb two years ago when "Rube" graduated from that worthy institution and came to the University. We should worry, though, we were the gainers. Her two years spent with us have convinced those, who weren't already convinced, that jovialness and congeniality with a sweet smile make us glad we are living. Her scholarship and her active part in college activities predict a great future—one that her Alma Mater can justly be proud of.

name: ROBERT HENRY McRAE
motto: "I am seldom indignant, and never surprised."
description: Not given to seriousness nor to frivolity, not aspiring too high lest the fall be greater, and not pursuing too diligently his studies, but given to thinking and to solving, in his own way, the problems as they occur, is characteristic of Mac. We see him with a smile and a song on his lips, this denoting a congenial disposition and an optimistic spirit which is quickly recognized by every one. Again he is noticed with a pensive, wistful expression as he gazes into the distant. There is, no doubt, concealed within his breast, ambition, desire, and yearning, which we surely hope, and believe he will attain.

name: MALCOLM NASH, B.S.C.
motto: "Determination and Faith in God will win for any man."
description: "Papa" Nash has spent four full years at Georgia—years profitable in friendships, studies, and general acquaintance with life. The politicians of the campus have had more names on their glib tongues, but Pop has formed only the true and lasting kind of friendships; other students have surpassed him in reputation for scholarship, but "Dad" has been a consistent worker without dwarfing his college days by being a slave to books and grades. As Malcolm goes into the sterner school of Life, we hope that he will get all that he deserves, all that his career with us has shown that he merits. From the Goddess of Fortune he only asks Justice; he doesn't need Mercy.

name: THOMAS MAUDIT NELSON, JR., B.S.C.
motto: "Live so that you can look any man in the eye."
description: A sociable athlete, one who can have his picture in the Sunday papers, or his name in streamers, without getting high hat. For that's the funny part of it, he doesn't wear a hat. In the Spring, when a young man's fancy turns to co-eds, you can see this fellow all dressed up, and if you'll look a little closer you'll find a young lady in the neighborhood. Tommy will never have much trouble in living, for he likes it.

name: GWINN HUXLEY NIXON, B.S.
motto: "It is written."
description: Why Gwinn desires that this laconic bit of Oriental fatalism be announced as his philosophy is a mystery.
