But this much is true: It must have been written that he was destined to do big things in college, for there are few who reach the Olympian heights that Gwinn attained. Modest and retiring, almost to a fault, he has been the sought and not the seeker.

name: BURWELL ATKINSON NOLAN, B.S.C.
motto: "We are alive; therefore, why worry about it?"
description: The good-looking man above is Burwell Atkinson Nolan, known to the ladies as "Brown-Eyes," and to one particular female as "Mr. B. A. Burhead." Burwell is a boy of magnetic personality. During his four years here he has endeared himself to no small group of boys because of his honesty, integrity, and high moral ideals. After Father Time has left his stamp upon our brow, we, his friends, are confident of finding him where he should be, a success among the successful.

name: HUGH PENDLETON NUNNALLY, B.S.C.
motto: "Never work, but always play; That's the way to spend the day."
description: A sportsman, and a gentleman. In the field of play, Hugh has always acquitted himself in such a manner that he has always gained the admiration and respect of his teammates and his opponents. His teammates have shown their admiration and respect by twice electing him captain of the University golf team. In the social field Hugh's quiet and unassuming manner, coupled with a pleasant disposition, have served to make a winning personality that has made his companionship not only always welcome, but often sought.

name: ALVIN LEE ODOM, D.V.M.
motto: "Worry not over the future; the present is all thou hast. For the future will soon be present, and the present will soon be past."
description: After serving honorably and faithfully in the World War, in the year of 1922 Alvin came to the University from his domicile in the little town of Lyons. His permanent wave and pleasing personality immediately gained him many friends. True to his convictions, he has stamped himself as a diligent student, a loyal friend, and a man. Alvin, our best wishes will follow you throughout your life, and should the wheel of fortune turn in the way you so richly deserve, your life will be one of happiness and boundless success.

name: DOUGLAS MILTON ORR, A.B.
motto: "To the man of good intelligence, hail!"
description: Athens born—and Athens should be proud of one of her sons who so efficiently reached the end of his college years! He has distinguished himself as a good student, a sportsman, and a musician. His friends are too numerous to count, and he is a real one himself. A strong character, a good fellow; we wish him the best of luck.

name: LOUISE BACON OSBORNE, A.B.J.
motto: "From sleek contentment keep me free, and fill me with a buoyant doubt."
description: Steady, easy-going, dependable are qualities choice and rare; Louise has them all. She is a girl admired and loved by all. She is always willing to help others in anything. She holds the highest ideals and lives up to them. Watch Louise—we expect much from her. She has great ability which promises great success. To us with Louise two things stand like stone: Kindness in another's trouble, courage in her own.

name: MALCHUS COBB OWEN, B.S.A.
motto: "Life is not measured by accumulations of wealth and years, but by service to country."
enrolltime: one year before he helped Uncle Sam lick the Germans
description: "Cobb," as he is known to his host of friends, has had a wonderful career. He came to Georgia one year before he helped Uncle Sam lick the Germans. After serving several months in France, he went home, married, and started teaching school. We are glad you came back to us, Cobb, and hope that your life's work will be the success your second stay at Georgia has been.

name: HUBERT BOND OWENS, B.S.A.
motto: "To have a friend, you must be one."
description: Hubert has not just occupied space during his four years here at the University, but in military and scholarly circles he has made himself a familiar figure. His activities are not confined to these two, however, but his forensic ability has carried him far toward success, not to mention his tact with the fair sex! The only index to the future is the records of the past, and judging from the latter, only the brightest career awaits him.

name: NORMA CLAIRE PARKER, A.B., Ed.
motto: "To do what I think best, and to do it well."
description: Norma, "The Individual One." Here we find one of those rare persons who dares to be herself. Norma Claire is small, light-haired, brown-eyed—and self-willed. She is a good student and does her work willingly. She is generous, alert, interested and interesting. Always willing to help others, she seldom needs others' help. She does not seek to impose her personality on others, but only claims the right to assert her individuality; she never begs, but always obtains the admiration, respect and love of all who are fortunate enough to know and understand her.

name: JAMES RUEY PATRICK, M.A.
motto: "The active virtues of citizenship are self-reliance, initiative, and originality."
description: "Pat" is a thoughtful, speculative person who comes as near having the real student's spirit as anyone we have ever seen. He is slow and thorough, with a fine sense of values. Although he does not allow his judgment to be swayed by personal prejudices, he is human enough to have these prejudices, which keeps him from being a pedant. If he does not wear himself out working, he will amount to something big, for he is intelligent, kindly, and as devoid of smallness as it is possible for anyone to be.

name: CELIA PERRY
motto: "Study to show thyself approved unto God, a workman that needeth not to be ashamed, rightly divining the word of truth."
description: "For years she grew in sun and shower, And nature said, 'A lovelier flower On earth was never sown; This girl I to myself will take; She shall be mine and I will make A lady of my own.'" And so she has grown, 'till now nature's lady stands, a college graduate, sweet, winsome, beautiful, and beloved by all who know her.

name: B. HENRY PETERSON
motto: "Work by formulae—only in business."
description: "Pete," though a two-year man, has made many friends through his jovial and hearty-well-met manner. For this future pharmacist, who is an earnest and searching student, we can only see success. Pete has already propounded many patent medicines of his own in the University laboratories, and we feel sure we shall see many "Peterson Compounds" in the future. Wide-awake, energetic, and industrious, he works diligently to attain this end. But Pete doesn't let his college activities exclude him from the social circle, where he is widely known.

name: SARAH ELIZABETH PETTY
motto: "Neatness is a crowning grace of womanhood."
clubs: Chairman, Publicity Committee, Y. W. C. A., '24-'25
description: Sarah came to us in 1923. Though she has been here only three years, her charming personality has made many and lasting friends. She has made an enviable record at the University by her conscientious and dependable work. When a thing must be done Sarah can be depended upon to carry it out. Though her ambition is to teach school, we can better imagine her in the little bungalow that she became so interested over in "44." We will miss you, Sarah; yet, we know you are well prepared.

name: EMMA ELIZABETH PLASTER, A.B.
motto: "When buttermilk is made, a churn will make it."
description: Those who do not know Emma have missed a pleasure. Apparently, she is quiet and studious. Her work would lead others to believe that she studies constantly, for she is an A. class student. But when you know her, you discover that she is full of fun. To be with her is a pleasure. Another index of her character is that she is liked by girls and boys alike. When an attractive girl is liked by girls, you may be sure that there is more to her than mere affectation.

name: HARRY POE, D.V.M.
motto: "To perform each worthy task as if the whole world were depending upon its proper accomplishment."
enrolltime: coming to the Classic City in 1920
description: "Edgar Allen" is one of those steady, quiet, unassuming fellows who can always be counted on as a loyal friend. He is a product of Vidalia, Georgia, coming to the Classic City in 1920, but was kept from college the two following years. He shows the ability to stick to a task until it is finished, which he has shown all through his college course. He is also a surgeon of note; he can give an anaesthetic that is very lasting. Harry, we hate to see you go, but predict a success in your chosen field.

name: MYRON ISAAC PRISANT, B.S.C.
motto: "Let me be happy as long as I live, and live as long as I am happy."
description: Myron is one of those persons who can laugh at tragedy, yet show signs of sympathy when sympathy is needed. A mutual murder by a couple of fools would double him up with merriment, but he'd break his neck finding milk for a hungry kitten. During four years, Myron has learned all the niceties of style, but he isn't a fashion model, for he often uses his head for more intricate things than balancing a hat.

name: JEWELL BLAKE PULLEN, B.S.A.
motto: "My only books were woman's looks, and folly's all they've taught me."
description: Blake, whose native heath is Heard county, came to the University in the fall of '23 from the Carrollton A. & M. By dint of hard work and diligent application, he has completed the four-year B. S. A. course in three years. Mirth, optimism and verbosity constitute the nature of our friend Pullyan. Needless to say, Blake is quite a beau-brummel, having a particular fondness for titian-haired femmes. By virtue of what we know of Blake—his character, personal ability, and educational attainments—we predict a great success for him in his chosen field.

name: GEORGE BUFORD RANDOLPH, B.S.
motto: "Not orthodoxy, but honesty."
description: In September, 1922, a tall, flaxen-haired and smiling young gentleman from Dalton entered the University of Georgia. For four years, George Randolph has quietly and unostentatiously acquired learning, and in addition has made many friends. Congeniality and altruism are the soul of this man. Taking the B. S. course, George has achieved fame as a physics shark, having taken every course in that science offered by the University. He is a well-known equestrian, too, as evidenced by his record on the cavalry field. Here's success to you, George—a noble gentleman and a true friend.

name: HARVEY WALTER RANKIN, B.S.A.
motto: "A fool and his college are soon parted."
description: A triune, dynamic compactness, dissolved as body, personality and pipe, collectively emanating biological philosophy. A complete student, having attained high grades, but caring mostly for integrity of opinion. A diversity of capabilities, manifested in a compelling congeniality. A delightful comrade for lovers of truth.

name: MATTIE JANE RIDGWAY, B.S.H.E
motto: "Truth is stranger than fiction—also more decent."
description: Little did we think three years ago, when Mattie first registered here, that she would prove to be such an interesting person. From that day she has been in the center of every event at the "Co-Ed Barn." While her chief accomplishment, probably, is her ability to hold an audience spellbound, still she has others quite as interesting, of which her sympathetic disposition is not the least. She has helped many a person over a rough road by her kind advice, and is ever ready to do her bit to make the world better and brighter.

name: CLIFFORD NOLEN RICHARDSON, A.B.J.
motto: "It's not whether you won or lost, but how you played the game."
description: The final chapter of a meteoric career will be written as the curtain drops upon Nolen Richardson's stay at the University. Nolen is one of the most distinguished athletes ever to wear Red and Black spangles, and is a prince among men. In addition to his athletic ability, Nolen has displayed a most coveted ability to make friends, not only among his classmates, but among all with whom he came in contact.

name: THOMAS S. RUSSELL, JR., B.S.C.
motto: "My philosophy is to refrain from philosophizing."
description: If the aim of a college is to impart a knowledge of life, breadth of character, charm of manner, and the ability to move easily among a host of friends, it has succeeded admirably in this instance. His knowledge of life and ideals are of the finest type, his character impeccable, his manner such that he numbers his friends by the Student Directory—friends in whose memory he will linger long after the superficial acquaintances of college days have been relegated to forgetfulness.

name: MABEL SANDERS, A.B.
motto: "Slight not what is near through seeking that which is far."
description: A persevering and sweetly disposed "Belle" is she. Her smiles win her friends, and her sincerity keeps them. No one could be her enemy, for she never says or does anything except what is kind. She would rather greet a new-comer to the University than to eat fudge candy (and she is certainly fond of the latter). Cheerfully and thoroughly done is the secret of her success. She would not acknowledge that she knew anything if her head of curly hair were threatened. Although her talents are hidden under modesty, they creep out and will make for her the place that she deserves. Just watch, Georgia will be proud of her.

name: JOHN B. SATLOF, B.S.C.
motto: "Like the moon; silent, yet sees and knows all."
clubs: Runner-up in Georgia State Tennis Tournament, '24, '25
description: "Sat" has made a wonderful record and has earned himself an enviable reputation in athletics as well as scholastic endeavors. Because of his earnest character and pleasing personality, he has made many friends. We know that "Sat's" career will be productive, and many worthy purposes of life will be accomplished. Go to it "Sat"—your Alma Mater is watching you.

name: ROBERT TAYLOR SEGREST, B.S.C.
motto: "Be clever, and let those who can't, be good."
description: Bob is a terror to those freshmen who know him as Professor, but to those who are associated with him outside of his official capacity, he is an insouciant gourmand. Not indifferent in a scholastic sense—far from it—Fate has seen fit to shroud him with all its vestals of leadership—academically and socially. Bob's knowledge of the modern world is non-pareil. Pearls of wit and wisdom from his well-exercised lips are hungrily sought after by his fellows.

name: WALTER EDWIN SEWELL
motto: "Make ambition tread before, and let not your pace slacken toward its attainment."
clubs: Captain, Infantry, R. O. T. C., '24-'25
President, Student Chapter, American Society of Civil Engineers
description: "Doc" has proven himself an "ace" in everything he has undertaken—an efficient scholar, a man's man, and a real friend. What more can be said, except: "We hate to lose him."

name: HORACE DeWITTE SHATTUCK
motto: "Work and a clear conscience are passwords anywhere."
clubs: Captain, Company "A," R. O. T. C.
description: A gentleman to rise above any situation, with wide ranging abilities his grim tenacity of purpose has carried him to varied successes in the classroom and on the athletic field. A truer Christian or stauncher friend never blessed the lives of men.

name: STEPHEN PARKS SHELL
motto: "The determination to succeed is the first step to success."
description: Here we have a young man with clean thoughts, a noble ambition, and a desire to make the world a better place in which to live. He has very unassuming manners, and has supreme confidence in his fellow-men until they prove that they are unworthy of it. He can only do his Alma Mater good, for his disposition will not let him do otherwise. His college friends wish for him success in his field of work.

name: CECIL WYMAN SHERLOCK, B.S.C.
motto: "If you would have a faithful servant, and one that you like, serve yourself."
description: "Ikey" entered the University in the fall of 1923, coming to us from the academically renowned prep school—Richmond Academy. He entered as a freshman, but by hard work and diligent studying, was able to graduate in three years. Sherlock is an athlete of no mean ability, having starred for two years on both the varsity football and baseball teams. He says that he will be back for another year of athletics and post-graduate work. We hope so.

name: WILLIAM HARRIS SIMPSON, JR., B.S.A.
motto: "Always strive to do bigger and better things; a man cannot stand still; he must either go forward or backward."
description: "Still water runs deep," was the sage reflection of one of the ancient philosophers that is very applicable in the case of Harry. Of quiet demeanor, yet there is something about him that shows him to be very much in touch with the world at large. Harry is a product of the "Classic City." Having lived here all of his life, he has absorbed much of its atmosphere. He has chosen the field of agriculture for his life work. His training well fits him for this.

name: LAMAR COBB SLEDGE, B.S.C.E.
motto: "People who live in glass houses—"
clubs: Vice-President, Student Chapter, American Society of Civil Engineers, '25-'26
description: I am writing this myself, so expect almost anything.

name: ALLEN N. SMITH
motto: "You are never whipped until you quit; don't quit."
description: "Blackshear" has been with us four years, and we have enjoyed his stay. Always a true gentleman, he early won his way into the hearts of all who came into contact with him and "he will be gone but not forgotten!" In all branches of student activities he has constantly strived and justly he has been honored by the many honors conferred upon him in athletic as well as other lines. To know him is to love him and we predict for "Blackshear" a career crowned with success in any endeavor he may undertake. When praises are sung to those leaving the University this year, let us not forget this true Georgia son, one who has been fired with spirit in everything beneficial.

name: JOHN FLETCHER SMITH
motto: "Opinions can be tolerated only when facts are lacking."
description: Here is an unique character; smoothly sarcastic, however, not given to cynicism; serious to a degree, but still quixotic. He postulates nothing, but will listen to an argument with patience. Unless the subject is intelligently handled, the premises will be shattered with few words, and curt. All things do not look good and bright to him, neither gloomy. He lives in the twilight. If a rain is heard tapping on a tin roof, he calculates that the grass is getting wet. If a piano is to be moved, he is not the man to grab the stool. A strong mind, body, and spirit will be in the engineering profession at an early date.

name: LEONA SMITH
motto: "Service."
description: Several girls were gathered in a "bull session" in the Co-Ed Barn. Talk dragged. Another joined the party, and screams of mirth immediately followed. That characterizes "Red" with her wealth of fun and wit, and her inevitable good humor. And then she is dependable—the backbone of everything in which she takes a part. In spite of demure qualities she nerved herself to join the Forestry Department. Square dances are "Red's" specialty. She never misses one. Yet, somehow, she always found time to make an excellent record.

name: Martha Elizabeth Smyth
motto: "Everything is beautiful, why make it otherwise?"
description: We all know her, and we don't. She came here in '24, determined to do three years in two—in one of the University's most difficult courses. Although she persists in leading a hermit's life, rumors of her ability along aesthetic lines lead us to feel that we have missed something by her not taking part in our Dramatic and Glee Club. In her ambition to be a costume designer, we know that she will make good. Her striking appearance speaks too loud for failure. We only wonder what the styles will be then.

name: Marilla Elizabeth Straight
motto: "Early to bed and early to rise makes a person a good Rotarian."
enrolltime: After two years at Piedmont College
description: After two years at Piedmont College, Elizabeth came to Georgia to continue her scholastic career. Although she has never let studying interfere with her "University Education," Elizabeth has made a splendid record, a number of friends, and has had a jolly good time all the while. The best thing we can say is that we hope her future will be as successful as the memory which she leaves with her friends is pleasant.

name: Hugh Smiley Stanley
motto: "A miss is as good as she wants to be."
description: We liked Hugh from the start because of his modest, yet friendly manner. Closer contact with him has brought out traits of character which demand our admiration as well as love. A capable mind and a spirit which refuses to follow the paths of least resistance, have accounted for his enviable academic record and signal honors in the R. O. T. C.

name: CHARLES HOUSTON STARLING, JR.
motto: "All work and no play makes 'Jack'."
clubs: Student Chapter, American Society of Civil Engineers
description: Those who have known "Shorty" need no discussion of his qualities. To you who have yet the pleasure, here is a man in whom all confidence can be safely placed; a worker who has the determination to turn his ideals into facts. A real friend, a scholar, and a gentleman. Luck to you, Shorty.

name: PINCKNEY ALSTON STEINER
motto: "What's all the hurry? Let's stop, be lazy, and enjoy ourselves."
description: Augusta proudly claims this well-loved and illustrious senior. Since his shadow first darkened the entrance arch to the University campus four years ago, "Pinck" has grown, surely and steadily, into a man of the first degree. During his stay here he has broken the scholastic record in his Department, and not only has he shown brilliance in his studies, but other activities have felt the results of his interest.

name: JAMES LOUIS STEPHENS
motto: "First we must have self-respect, if we are to be respected by others."
description: "Cowboy," philosopher, poet, musician, southern gentleman, and hard to please; what more could be said? We all know him as "Cowboy." He has made a scholastic record of which any one would be proud. He is specific in his statements, deep in his thoughts, and stands true to his convictions. When he makes a statement, you may feel assured that it is well thought and clearly made. He is the kind of friend a fellow likes to have at all times.

name: SARAH LOUISE STEWART
motto: "Keep thy heart with all diligence; for out of it are the issues of life."
description: Did you ever meet a tall brunette, attractive, friendly and gay; with laughing eyes and a happy smile, who made music for your ears, who was fluent in speech, inspiring to associate with, and a comrade in all the word implies? Then it was Sarah. "She was a phantom of delight when first she gleamed upon my sight."

name: NELL MARIE STOREY
motto: "First be sure of yourself; then go ahead."
description: Marie—she can bake a cake or play a game of basketball equally as well. She can make a dress or lead a square dance. In fact, Marie can do anything, but best of all, she likes doing things for other people. She is unselfish, cheerful and dependable. She is never known to be in a bad humor; she is the life of any party. Marie does happy little things for people and doesn't tell the world about it. She is studious and not a dig; she is a leader and not aggressive; she is a good sportsman and a true friend.

name: EDNA ELIZABETH STRAUSS
motto: "She never mourns a mischief that is gone But plans a way to bring new mischief on."
description: This distinguished blond hails from the municipality of Augusta. If she will, she will, you may depend on't. If she won't she won't, and there's an end on't. Edna believes in running off with the honors of the University. Her winning personality itself made for her an unending line of friends. She always stood for those things which tend to make the University a better one. Everybody knows there is a little bit of bad in every good little girl.

name: GRACE EVELYN STRAUSS, A.B.
motto: "She is always laughing, never sad, Sometimes naughty but never bad."
clubs: Undergraduate Representative, Y. W. C. A., '25-'26
description: Stand back! Hold your lids, and let the whirl-wind pass. It is only Grace blowing in from Augusta, where she has come off with honors. At the University, honors have come to her, but she did not deliberately seek them. They came as a natural result of worthy efforts. Grace has a very winning personality, and this, together with her savoir faire, drew the admiration of all those with whom she associated.

name: HERMAN PERRY SULLIVAN, A.B.J.
motto: "Women like men. Men like women. Women, like men, like a good time."
description: The cackling of geese saved Rome from being surprised by an invading enemy, a field mouse made a poet of Robert Burns, and a fifteen-cent sugarbowl at an auction sale made a hero of H. P. Sullivan. An exponent of reality and a student of human nature, his books have not been his only teachers. He studies men and things with those steel-blue eyes of his; and to the world into which he goes we give warning that no sort of sham or artificiality can blindfold him.

name: COKE WELLS TALMADGE, B.S.C.
motto: "What's worth doing at all is worth doing well."
description: The big, bad, Bull is just plain Coke Talmadge now. "Goobie" in two years at the University acquired a reputation for recklessness and ferociousnesss that gained for him the title of the "Wild Bull of the Pampas," and well did he deserve this title. Yes, boys, it's the old, old story. Our Goobie fell in love and is now a model for any ambitious boy. He attends classes faithfully, and walks the straight and narrow way, in every manner.

name: JULIUS GLENN TATUM, B.S.C.
motto: "Each for all and all for each."
description: J. G. served in the World War, and later came to the University, where he has overcome many difficulties, getting his degree in three years, and at the same time winning the respect and esteem of many people. Honest, upright, and always a gentleman, Tatum has fought his way valiantly. Ever ready and willing to do a friendly deed, always sincere in his actions, he is one whom everyone is glad to have known. Tatum is a steady, hard-working boy, and possesses marked ability, a wonderful disposition, and highest morals of the truest type. We prophesy for him a life full of service, happiness and success.

name: DUDLEY HILTON TAYLOR, A.B.J.
motto: "I desire that acts, not words, shall speak for me."
description: Dudley Taylor, or "Dud," as he is better known by his friends, is no "Dud" at all, but a corking good student, sometimes reporter for the Banner-Herald of Athens, Red and Black, and ever at the hue and cry of another person who knows him a little too well for comfort. If you must know who it is that knows so much on "Dud"—it's Mrs. Dudley. A man who can keep a wife and get a dip at the same time deserves whatever he gets and he usually gets it.

name: EXEKIEL FRED THOMAS, D.V.M.
motto: "Your ability is shown by the results you obtain."
description: In the fall of 1922, came to us from Franklin county, a mere lad with high ideals, an able student and a conscientious worker. He is willing to make any sacrifice in order to attain a worthy end. He believes that a man gets out of a thing only that which he puts into it; and in consequence everything that he undertakes, from the simplest to the hardest task, always receives the same careful preparation. He was not so popular with the fair sex as he might have been, but he is loved and admired by many.

name: ARNOLDINA THORNTON
motto: "Give the best to the world, and the best will come back to you."
enrolltime: two years ago from Agnes Scott
description: Dina came to the University two years ago from Agnes Scott, and Georgia gained by her coming. Open-hearted, generous, always ready to help, she is as warm and loyal a friend as can be found. She is a real girl, too, taking everything with a smile, yet underneath, sincere and conscientious. Is it any wonder that we all love her? Her's is the nature which makes friends. Her future is destined to be as cheerful as her college days.

name: HELEN TREANOR
motto: "Fare on fair way-farer, and may other laurels await thee."
description: With a stick of dynamite attached and automatically ignited, as it were, at intervals during her college career Helen would have dimmed the stars. But doing four years in three was quite enough effort to sad Earth, without stirring up trouble in the heavens. Pull away her mask of indifference and apparent lack of ambition, and never was anyone with more real initiative, generosity and unselfishness. Meeting obstacles with unruffled poise, keeping her objective ever foremost, she has forged steadily toward her goal, passing it with the unobtrusive ability which is one of her outstanding characteristics.

name: SAMUEL YOER TUPPER, JR.
motto: "Live in the present As long as it's pleasant, The future's a refuge for gloom."
description: Anyone who knows Sam will see the impossibility of doing him justice in a hundred words. Sam is a genius at reading, writing, and, (no, not arithmetic, quite the converse) talking. He is desirable wherever he goes because of his attractive mannerisms, brilliant conversation and scintillating wit. A person who supplants boredom by laughs and does more than his share for the happiness of the world.

name: JOHN GIBSON VAN HOUTEN, B.S.A.
motto: "Argue not; it wasteth the breath; never-the-less, have thine own way."
description: Words have never been adequate for expressing the real value of a man, so to state the qualities of Jack would fill innumerable pages and still be lacking. Let it be sufficient, then, to say that those who have known him realize his strength of character, his determination of purpose, and his loyalty to the right thing at all times. A gentleman and a true friend—May your life be filled with happiness, well deserved. Luck to you, Jack.

name: LILLIAN LOUISE WADE, A.B.J.
motto: "Count him but a fool who follows the herd."
description: Honors, of which she received many, served merely to grace her genial and virile personality. Neither sheer optimist nor gloomy pessimist—neither hedonist nor idealist nor practicalist—she combined qualities of kindness, good humor and sound intelligence with a buoyant activity and personal charm. She led because she possessed the spirit of a leader. To be admired, to be liked, to be loved—that was to be Lillian.

name: FRANCES WALTON, A.B., Ed.
motto: "I haven't decided yet."
clubs: Sponsor, R. O. T. C. Unit
description: Frances is essentially an athletic girl. Not only can she do the Charleston, but she likes all forms of athletics, and is particularly in love with football and those who play it. She attends every game, and has a smacking good time. Still, her personality is not limited to athletics; she has charms for all. Popular alike in her fraternity, on the campus and in the ballroom, she will be remembered by her friends here as well as by the many she is certain to make after she leaves college.

name: ELEANOR WARD
motto: "To thine own self be true."
description: Eleanor came to the University in the fall of 1924 from Rhinehardt College. With only thirteen hours of college credit, Eleanor entered the Sophomore class with a strong determination to finish in two years. With her strong intellectual powers, originality, and her willingness to apply herself, she succeeded in finishing in two years. During this time, her magnetic personality has won for her many friends. Eleanor, to the joy of those who know her, has a very rare ability—that of understanding human nature. This, together with her high sense of humor, certainly helps to make this old world a happier, better place in which to live.

name: MRS. EDITH O'STEEN WARD
motto: "Eat, drink, and be merry, for tomorrow we may die."
description: Edith is quite partial to educational institutions maintained by the state, having attended G. S. C. W. before coming to the University. Knowing her at Georgia, the embodiment of fun and vivacity, one would never suspect that she had shouldered the responsibilities of a county agent for two years. Edith is decidedly the athletic type with a fondness for the great out-of-doors. She wields both hammer and saw with facility in a constructive way. Characteristic of her happy disposition, is her favorite expression, "Would you worry?" Anybody whom she calls "friend" has been tried and found "all there."

name: WALTER PRESTON WARREN, JR.
motto: "Smile at the world, and it will smile back at you."
description: Someone has said, "Manhood, not Scholarship, is the first aim of education." Walter has attained both of these aims so we may truly say his education is complete. He is a brilliant and persevering student, having attacked some of the hardest courses in the curriculum with a zeal that is astounding. He has carried on his work quietly without any of the brass-band tactics, and has emerged from a three-year struggle still among the ranks of those who love the golden mean. We have watched him grow from a boy in the short time he has been here, casting aside his puerilities and taking on some of those characteristics that so clearly denote manhood. Walter is a linguist supreme. We sincerely hope that some of those precepts which he has learned from Greek will be a guiding hand in his future life.

name: EDGAR ARTHUR WAXELBAUM
motto: "Only while seeking after Beauty and Truth shall I find real Happiness."
description: Although Edgar once posed as a model in a Fashion Show of What the Correctly-dressed Young Man Will Wear, he is not at all a model young man. The model young man is self-assured, brisk, practical, assertive. But Edgar is still in the groping stage, is quiet and a dreamer, is above all an idealist who has seen just enough of the world not to know whether to be amused or embittered . . . . The years ahead will make interesting watching.

name: JAMES VERNON WEBB
motto: "There are two foundations for personal enlightenment—few words, and fewer words."
description: This man has won honors under handicaps. He is one among few who think. The problem once solved, he will stand for his convictions, yet encourages argument so that he may see the truth from all angles. A leading student, outstanding in college activities; balances the theoretical and practical so that when harnessed together, the maximum results are obtained. Arranging others' ideas, comparing with his own, Polishing the rugged diamond into the precious stone.

name: RUTH E. WEIDEMAN
motto: "Be tolerant"
description: A will-o'-the-wisp from Wisconsin! But wait—I have given you the wrong impression! Ruth is an unusual combination of the light-hearted and the serious. She is of the happiest and the gayest in a laughing throng; she is of the most earnest and interested of students. It was a mark of wisdom after three years at "Wisconsin" to spend her fourth college year in the hospitable South. She has learned our ways; we have learned her ways, and we love her.

name: GLENN FREDERICK WILEY, B.S.A.
motto: "Ideals without attainment mean little."
description: "Glenn Frederick." The name indicates royal ancestry. At college he conducts himself as a well-bred college man should, but during vacation often resorts to acts of violence, tarring and feathering being his specialties—A Ku Klux, we believe. Foolishness aside, Glenn is a true friend. To do a favor and tell a good joke are his delights. He has won highest honors, which is enough said. Glenn, we believe in you. You have ability and the world before you. Make good.

name: CLARK REID WILLCOX, B.S.A.
motto: "A long pull and a hard one brings success."
clubs: Commander, Joe Brown Connally Chapter, No. 2, D. A. V.
description: It is a pleasure to write about a man with the character, ideals, energy, and ability that this man has. Willcox, in whose mouth is found no guile, is most considerate of the other man's feelings and desires. After serving in the World War, he came to the University to complete his education, and has made a record of which we are proud. May he spend his life among those who are deserving of such friendship.

name: THOMAS EDWARD WINSLOW, JR., B.S.C.
motto: "Kindness and consideration is within itself a religion."
description: Men of words have come and gone, but here is a silent performer, one who does his work well, quietly and unassuming, in a gentlemanly and courteous manner which has won friends for him from every walk of college life. Here's hoping that he has the best of luck and a generous slice of happiness, well deserved and honorably won.

name: SAMUEL WASDEN WOOD, A.B., Ed.
motto: "Love, Honor, and Truth—my precepts."
description: This handsome young collegian, who hails from New York, Miami—and Sandersville, has won for himself a distinct position in all phases of University life, and besides possessing a most genial disposition, Sam has the quality of leadership and an innate desire to succeed in all things he undertakes. Sam is an ardent lover of the aesthetic and at the same time has the peculiar power to couple this with an intense fondness for sports, being a skillful pianist and having been Manager of the Varsity Football team.

name: SAM H. WORLEY, A.B.J.
motto: "Moderation in all things."
description: "A gentleman and a scholar,"—this ancient platitude was turned for none other than Sam, for he represents a happy mingling of two contradictory traits, combining a genial personality with a penchant for study that makes of him another argument for the Henry W. Grady School of Journalism. He is by nature pleasure-loving, but it is so curbed by a keen consciousness of service and by an ingrained industry as to make him an all-around good fellow. Whatever he takes up as his life's work he will be a success, and if loyalty, persistence, and industry mean anything he will go far—and stay there.

name: DOROTHY DANIEL WRIGHT, A.B.
motto: "Of your unspoken word you are master—your spoken word is master of you."
description: Montreat gave up her best when she gave up Dorothy. In the opinion of all her associates no one is more energetic, and conscientious than she, or has a sweeter, more lovable personality. Her friends are numbered by her acquaintances, and she is such a true friend that many seek her acquaintance. Dorothy has made a splendid record at the University and won the love and respect of faculty and students.

name: IVY WILSON WRIGHT, A.B.J.
motto: "Freedom of the human spirit is the thing that matters most of all."
description: Wounded in the World War and with health undermined. Ivy came to the University handicapped. He did not seek for honors, but with moderate effort he proved himself to be a superior student—had health permitted, he could have achieved the highest outward rank. In all important student activities, his advice was sought. Reserved, thoughtful, he made few intimate friends; he had the high respect of all who knew him, teachers and students alike.

name: JOHN HARRISON WRIGHT, B.S.A.
motto: "A good name is rather to be chosen than great riches."
description: Most of us would be proud of John's honors alone, but not so with him. Besides honors, John has made a good scholastic record, and a social record that would put to awe any socialist. To know John is one thing; to room with him is another.

name: MURRAY ELLIS WYCHE, A.B.J.
motto: "He who only hopes is hopeless."
clubs: Lieutenant R. O. T. C., Infantry
description: It is men like Murray that make Georgia a leader among colleges. During his four years stay here he has made a record that is enviable in every respect. However, it was not a desire for personal fame that has made him a leader on the campus. Rather he has worked for the glory of the University, and through this has gained his own enviable reputation. In his chosen field of Journalism he cannot help but be a success,—and a credit to Georgia.

name: OSMOND REIMERT BIE
motto: "All the same, I doubt seriously whether the guy who said 'Stone walls do not a prison make, nor iron bars a cage' ever spent the night properly jailed."
description: Have you ever seen a man who told you by his walk that he didn't care if you liked him or not? That it really doesn't make any difference if the world turns from right to left or vice versa? That he'd as soon live in a pup tent as the White House? If you haven't, gaze upon him now. This walk and attitude has been characteristic of "Ozzy" ever since we've known him, and the strange part about it is that we like him for it.

name: PERRY BRANNEN
motto: "Live not for pleasure alone, but for the good that you may do."
description: Guyton is proud of its loyal son. During his stay here, Perry has made many friends who regret to see him leave the University. He has gained nearly every honor the law school has to offer. Congenial, sociable, diligent, quiet, friendly, and resourceful, Brannen possesses six qualities which will make him a man worthy of esteem, respect and friendship. Besides these, Perry bids fair to be a dependable man on the track squad this spring.

name: JOSEPH HEYWARD CHERRY
motto: "If you can't succeed, laugh at success."
description: Joe is a quiet, unassuming fellow, who through quietness and self-imposed obscurity has garnered a wealth of friendship. He is seldom found in the whirlpool of activity which surrounds University life; you'll find him on the edge of the disturbance picking up flotsam that happens his way. It is this flotsam that has made him wealthy. Such a life has given Joe time to think, which time he has not wasted, for he has developed a keen sense of observation along with a piercing conception of discrimination. We do not have to hope for Joe, fate looks out for the like of him.

name: LEWIS PERRY CHICK, LL.B.
motto: "If there's no oil in the lamp, the wick will be wasted."
enrolltime: Since his entrance into the University
description: Chick hails from Monroe, the anathema of hoboes. Since his entrance into the University, he has become one of the most popular members of the student body. By his successful prosecution of all cases coming into his hands in the Jeffersonian Moot Court of the Law School, Lewis has aptly demonstrated his thorough knowledge of law, his ability to assemble facts and arrive at the right conclusion therefrom,—the requisite qualities of a good lawyer. Whether Lewis follows his chosen profession, or enters some other field of endeavor, with his characteristic perseverance, he is certain to be successful.

name: ROBERT S. DENNIS, LL.B.
motto: "Give your ear to everyone, but your voice to few."
description: Presenting—Mr. "Pen" Dennis, a gentleman of no mean legal ability; a youth upon whom the gods have smiled. One who can wear his hat at a cocky angle and trample mere men with his dogmatism. This heir to past isms stands impregnably entrenched against modern fanatics, who raucously shout their stigmatic doctrines at his bolted door. A gullible, attractive in his gullibility, a fundamentalist who understands the antis, and refuses to adhere. An individual who will leave a lasting memory in the minds of those who have known him.

name: RICHARD ALDEN DENNY, JR., B.S.E.E., LL.B.
motto: "Knowledge is proud that it knows so much, while wisdom is humble that it knows no more."
description: Dick, having finished his course at Tech, entered Georgia in 1923. This year he graduates with a most creditable record, displaying unusual ability in legal research. His generous and congenial personality, which has won for him a host of friends, coupled with his diligence to duty, which has been so essential to his scholastic success, will surely gain for him an enviable position in his chosen profession.

name: JOHN PAXTON ERWIN, JR.
motto: "Know the truth, and the truth shall make you free."
description: Paxton entered the Lumpkin Law School in September, 1923, and for three years he has earnestly studied the law, his chosen profession. How well is attested by the fact that for the entire time he has been one of the leaders of his class, an honor well merited because of his untiring devotion to his studies, and his native ability to grasp the legal principles encountered. Paxton had two years of college training, and had taught for one year before entering Georgia. He likes to talk, is an Irishman, and will do anything for his friends. With these characteristics, we predict success for him as a lawyer.

name: EDWIN J. FEILER
motto: "Why all this toil for triumphs of an hour?"
description: Occasionally there flashes across the college horizon a career, brilliant as the course of a meteor. Such has been "Ed's." Endowed with a brilliant mind, his versatile activities and honors are proof of his worth. His friends, he numbers by Dean Dudley's absence list.

name: STARKEY SHARP FLYTHE
motto: "Smile, and someone else will smile in return."
description: "Stark" came into the Classic City from A. R. C., and decided to become a lawyer. His diligent study has proved his ability to become one of the outstanding lawyers of the state. His personality, together with his wit, has won for him many friends in the law school, especially among the co-eds; therefore, with the girls on his side, there ought to be a great future for him wherever he goes to practice law.

name: JOHN MORGAN FORRESTER
motto: "You will not go higher than you aim, therefore, let the sky be your limit."
description: Jack came into our midst from Georgia Tech. It took him about two days after his arrival to become known, and not more than three weeks to become a favorite with the boys. The study of law has been diligently pursued by him, and success, it seems, will be thrust upon him. Jack can be well termed the "Gentleman of the Class," and his striking, yet forceful personality will win for him opportunities that money cannot buy. His sagaciousness is bound to push him to the top in the legal profession.

name: LUCIUS GROVES
motto: "My greatest treasure, yet my greatest problem, is myself."
description: "Tiny's" name is as misleading as the "pork" in "pork and beans" or the "tasteless" in "tasteless castor oil." The metaphor is a little mixed, but so is "Tiny" - 200 versatile pounds of likable, happy-go-lucky humanity; one of those creatures who lean to the sunny side. "Versatile" because he is at once an athlete, a conscientious student, and "one of the boys." For three years he has been a dependable bulwark on the football squad, which he served at the post of tackle. He will be missed next fall, as the Bulldogs go coursing on the 1926 campaign.

name: EARL DUNBAR HARRISON
motto: "That school girl complexion is all right, but a few post-graduate additions don't do any harm."
description: The high regard held for Dunbar by his fellow students is expressed in the number of deserving honors that have been conferred upon him. "Dunny" has always proven himself to be a man of his word, and has always been prompt in performing whatever duty that was incumbent upon him. His genuine ability and promptness, along with his forcible traits of character, should, in the years to come, make him one of the most successful and popular members of the bar.

name: WILLIAM JULIAN HATCHER
motto: "Listen to others' ideas, but think for yourself."
description: "Wee Willie" is one of the best all-round fellows at the University of Georgia. Coming to us from Riverside in the fall of 1923, he has made a great record both in his studies and on the football field. A man of "Wee Willie's" record has a great future ahead. So here's hoping he will make one of the greatest practitioners of the age.

name: HENRY OLIN HUBERT
motto: "When in Rome, shoot roman candles."
description: Hither in the fall of 1921 came one Olin Hubert from Harlem, his native heath to quaff his thirst for knowledge. Possessed of a pleasing rotundity and a vast substantial smile, he garnered the good will of all who came in contact with him. A man of keen penetration, sound judgment, and an engaging sense of humor, he will no doubt achieve many laurels in his chosen profession—law. Widely read, astute, and possessing an unflinching will, he is well equipped for the trials of life. His two years of cultural training before taking up law place him a few strides ahead of most of his classmates. Good luck, "Fatty."

name: FREDERICK HEBER KENT
motto: "They only are wise who know that they know nothing."
description: Fred's pleasing personality, marked stability of character, and ability has enabled him to complete the course in law in less time than required and to achieve success and honor in social and scholastic activities.

name: DOROTHY IDA LEVY, LL. B.
motto: "I am true, for there are those who trust me; I am good, for there are those who care."
clubs: University Representative and Speaker at State Convention of League of Women Voters, '25
description: Diminutive "Dot," with the face of a Dresden Doll, and a mind of scintillating brilliance, leaves a record of achievements as signification of her ability, charm, and character.

name: JAMES EDWARD MANUCY, JR., LL.B.
motto: "Patient and persevering work is the only sure key to success? Piffle!"
description: It is probable that some Senior way back in the early days of college annuals conceived the idea of senior write-ups, and very likely this same chap started the familiar expressions seen in all annuals today. It's always, "He was the leader of the class;" "a bear with the ladies," and ending always with, "we wish him success in whatever he undertakes." Eddie has requested that nothing like that be said concerning him, though, he blushingly admits it to be true. "Manucy, from Savannah," has been a main attraction on the Glee Club for several years; is quite an artist on the saxophone, and a very close classroom friend of Dr. Sylvanus Morris.

name: FREEMAN CHEYNE McCLURE, A.B., LL.B.
motto: "Patient and persevering work is the only sure key to success."
clubs: Graduate Summa Cum Laude and Valedictorian, '23
description: Freeman began teaching his third and senior year, graduating with highest honors, a recognized leader with innumerable friends and the best known man in the University. The Georgia General Assembly has profited by his presence for six years. As a climax to a brilliant college career Freeman married one of Georgia's fairest daughters.

name: CHARLES JOSEPH O'BYRNE, A.B., LL.B.
motto: "Age quod agis."
enrolltime: After receiving an A. B. from Georgetown, Charlie meandered down to Athens.
description: After receiving an A. B. from Georgetown, Charlie meandered down to Athens. A short time later he became a marked, marked man, marked for popularity. There is a certain type of person whom one likes at the introduction and Charlie is in that category. Good looking, reserved but sociable, likable and intelligent. Charlie can entertain milady on Sunday, discourse with the intelligentsia on Monday, and be sociable with a peanut vendor any day of the week. But we don't believe he'll sell peanuts and pop-corn.

name: JOHN DUNCAN ODOM, LL.B.
motto: "Go, lose or conquer as you can, And be each, pray God, a gentleman."
description: Universitas Georgiae! You have lost the Lord Chesterfield of your campus. Ladies! Your Prince Charming has departed. My distinguished fellow citizens! Colonel John Duncan Odom is at your service. A brilliant collegiate career—social, political, and scholastic—is closed. Your Alma Mater has lost a favorite son, but the populace has gained a champion.

name: ARTHUR SEARS OLDHAM, LL.B.
motto: "Look out for the pennies and the dollars will go to the Co-op."
description: Arthur is the only native son of Athens in the class; he also has the distinction of being the "baby" of the class, though he stands high in scholarship and has made an excellent attendance record in the University. He is a well known church worker, especially along the line of Sunday School and Epworth League, in which he is an able speaker and worker. His quiet and unassuming manner wins for him many friends wherever he goes. Watch Oldham's smoke, he's bound to succeed.

name: AUSTIN HERSCHEL PARHAM, LL.B.
motto: "Too many enemies constitute a millstone around one's neck; true friendship is an idealist's conception; hence only a fool can assert independence."
description: Herschel, one of the youngest members of the class, has pursued the study of law with that unusual degree of success that comes only to the man with natural ability, remarkable comprehensiveness and keen interest. His innumerable friends acclaim for him, already, a life of usefulness and service. Upon graduation, he will go to an eastern university to complete his preparation for his life's work.

name: JOHN ROBERT PHILLIPS, JR., LL.B.
motto: "Experientia docet."
description: Four years at Washington and Lee left many traces but no scars, so John came to the University more like a finished product than a recruit. During his stay here he established himself as an Aurora Borealis among the luminaries of the Law School and made more friends than a political boss. In this short time he has entered and become prominent in every branch of student activity. John is an outstanding example of that rare individual whom the professors rate an excellent student and the students call a good boy.

name: WILLIAM TYRUS RAY, LL.B.
motto: "Let me live in a house by the side of the road and be a friend to man."
description: During "Bill's" 15 years as a "Knight of the Grip" and World Warrior, he has visited some dozen countries, which give this lawyer prospectus a claim to ultra-cosmopolitanism. Believing that right and wrong is a part of the law, and possessing qualities of conscientiousness, consistency, and punctuality are indications that clients will find hard work and loyalty in his counsel.

name: ERNEST PAUL ROGERS, LL.B.
motto: "My every effort shall be to so use mine own as not to infringe upon the rights of others."
description: "Jelly" has served his time in the law school, and together with picking up those things which are necessary to hang out the "Shingle," he has been a leading figure in the football world and in general student activities. As business manager of the Glee Club, "Jelly" has assumed the most difficult task connected with student activities and the result was the most successful Glee Club in the history of the University. "Jelly" has stood up for the things in which he believes and he is unceasing in his efforts to sustain those things. To sum him up is to say, and more cannot be said of any one, he is a MAN.

name: HERBERT A. STRICKLAND, LL.B.
motto: "When you are right, defy the world; when wrong, admit it."
clubs: Secretary and Treasurer of Sophomore Class
description: "Strick" is a man with a dynamic personality. He is admired by his many friends for his pluck and grit. Once your friend he is always your friend. He has taken much interest in collegiate activities, and is willing, at all times, to share his time with his friends, especially his lady friends. His accomplishments are varied and many.

name: HARRY L. TAYLOR, LL.B.
motto: "It pays to advertise."
description: Harry and Josiah Meigs came to Franklin College about the same time. That was when the Indians were a bit troublesome, they having had a hobby called scalping. Josiah became a little worried about the welfare of the students and called Harry into consultation. Whereat, Harry, being even then a witty fellow, thought up a scheme to fool the redskins. And that, dear children, is the history of hair-cutting. However, during the years that Harry has spent in Athens he has managed to get a couple of degrees and meet all the living graduates of the University. He is known from Blue Ridge to Tybee Light, there being no graduates in Rabun Gap.

name: ERNEST PAUL ROGERS, LL.B.
motto: "My every effort shall be to so use mine own as not to infringe upon the rights of others."

name: MATHILDE UPSON
motto: "Quod piffli shingorum booibus."
description: A destined lawyer for her state, destined to success and esteem, is Mathilde. Though socially popular, popularity has only firmly strengthened her ambitious pursuit of a lawyer's game. On the dance floor and "on the bench," Mathilde will draw her applause in life as she has done in college. Perhaps "some are born to be great;" if so, the gods have chosen her to all indications. A lover of her legal profession, a goal in view, and an ability to win—Mathilde embodies all. Best of wishes go with her from her many friends as she embarks her ship, "Career."

name: MINOR LEWIS WHEATON
motto: "All that glitters is not gold, But it's pretty while it lasts."
description: Minor began his collegiate career by attending the University of Virginia; however, he soon realized his error and returned to Georgia's fold. His ability as a songster has won for him a countless number of friends and admirers over the state, and his pleasing personality has made him more than popular on the campus. Minor, it has been a pleasure to know you, and it is with great reluctance that we see you leave us.

name: JULIAN DAVIS WOOTEN
motto: "Say little about what you are doing and you'll have plenty of time to do it."
description: If the Sphinx builders had lived in the twentieth century they could have found no better model than this Sylvesterite. Quietly he enters a conversation and the hotter it waxes the quieter he becomes. If words were pennies this fellow would be an ideal miser. But because a man is quiet does not mean that he is blind and dumb, far from it. "J. D." has spent three years at the University, and his marks are the highest in the Law School. He'll take his place in the world, and most probably while everyone else is jabbering, he'll silently take the lead.

name: IVER LEON WOOTEN, LL.B.
motto: "Apply your ability, and success is yours for the asking."
description: Three years have passed since "Woot" came to us from Quitman, one of Georgia's fairest cities. His friends have found him willing and ready to lend a helping hand. "Woot" is to be admired for his grim determination to do his task. As a fighter "Woot" is among the best. Although he was not a regular, he will long be remembered for his valuable service on the gridiron.

name: LUTHER MANDEVILLE WYATT, LL.B.
motto: "Plead not that cause wherein the tongue must be confuted by the conscience."
enrolltime: After attending Oglethorpe University two years
description: Having attended Oglethorpe University two years, Luther entered the University of Georgia thoroughly prepared to pursue the study of law. Because of his natural ability and brilliancy, he partakes with a chosen few the honor of being a class leader. An iron will and enviable character are his. With his aptness and alertness combined with that quality of perseverance, which is a part of him, he is destined to be a formidable foe in his legal encounters. Luther, upon his graduation will form a partnership with his brother, who is now practising law in LaGrange, Georgia.

name: LUTHER HARMON ZEIGLER, LL.B.
motto: "We are all to be rewarded according to our works, whether in punishment for evil, or blessings for good that we have done."
description: "Georgia" has claimed many of Savannah's sons, but none of them are more worthy than Luther. He chose wisely when he began the study of law, for he has clearly demonstrated his abilities to gain his point, and defeat his opponents in Moot Court. His diligent and persistent efforts have been rewarded, not only with well deserved college honors, but also with a comprehensive knowledge of law, which will gain distinction for him in years to come. Luther is not only a scholar, but he is also a gentleman of quiet and striking personality. Above all, he possesses a strong conception of honor. Gifted as he is with ability and determination, Luther will surely win fame in the legal profession.
